# Section 1: Introduction


1
00:00:00,760 --> 00:00:07,210
Now, before we dive into the course content, I'd like to give you a teaser or a taste of what you'll

2
00:00:07,210 --> 00:00:10,000
be able to do by the end of the course.

3
00:00:10,840 --> 00:00:17,740
Now, usually in my teaser lectures, I'd give one example to show students what they'll be able to

4
00:00:17,740 --> 00:00:19,690
do once done with the course.

5
00:00:19,900 --> 00:00:25,420
But in this course, you're going to learn so many cool things, and we're going to build more than

6
00:00:25,420 --> 00:00:27,490
20 hacking tools.

7
00:00:27,490 --> 00:00:32,650
So it's unfair, and it's really hard for me to pick only one example.

8
00:00:33,580 --> 00:00:40,060
Therefore, instead, I'm going to show you three examples taken from the three main sections of the

9
00:00:40,060 --> 00:00:40,720
course.

10
00:00:41,290 --> 00:00:47,170
Now, keep in mind each one of these sections is divided into a number of subsections.

11
00:00:47,320 --> 00:00:53,740
So these examples are only a small fraction of what you'll be able to do once done with the course.

12
00:00:53,740 --> 00:01:00,670
But I think they'll work really well to give you a taste of what you'll be able to achieve once done.

13
00:01:01,390 --> 00:01:06,420
Now, since this is just a teaser, don't worry about how these programs work.

14
00:01:06,430 --> 00:01:10,150
We will cover how to write these programs in details.

15
00:01:10,570 --> 00:01:14,920
So for now, just sit back, watch the lecture and enjoy it.

16
00:01:14,920 --> 00:01:20,740
And then once you start the course, you will learn how to write all of these programs and much, much

17
00:01:20,740 --> 00:01:21,370
more.

18
00:01:22,690 --> 00:01:26,680
In the first example, I'm going to use two programs.

19
00:01:27,070 --> 00:01:35,320
The first one will allow us to intercept data sent or received by any computer on the same network.

20
00:01:36,200 --> 00:01:44,060
The second one will read this data and filter it to show us usernames, passwords, visited URLs and

21
00:01:44,060 --> 00:01:44,840
so on.

22
00:01:44,870 --> 00:01:50,540
We're going to write these programs ourselves from scratch later on in the course.

23
00:01:51,500 --> 00:01:54,950
Now these programs are running on my hacker machine.

24
00:01:54,950 --> 00:02:01,670
So let's go to the target machine and try to generate some traffic and maybe log in to a service.

25
00:02:01,940 --> 00:02:06,260
I'm going to go to Hotmail dot com and let's try to sign in.

26
00:02:06,260 --> 00:02:11,180
So I'm going to click on sign in and I'm just going to put some sample information.

27
00:02:11,180 --> 00:02:17,060
So I'm going to put a username and the sample password and I'm going to hit enter.

28
00:02:17,860 --> 00:02:24,610
And now if we go back to the hacker machine, you can see that first of all, I can see all the requests,

29
00:02:24,610 --> 00:02:31,450
I can see all the websites they're visiting, and I can also see the username and password that they

30
00:02:31,450 --> 00:02:32,560
logged in with.

31
00:02:32,590 --> 00:02:39,700
So I can see the username was 822 at Hotmail dot com and I can see the password was one, two, three,

32
00:02:39,700 --> 00:02:40,930
four, five, six.

33
00:02:42,770 --> 00:02:46,610
The second example that I'm going to show you is a backdoor.

34
00:02:47,000 --> 00:02:54,320
A backdoor is a program that when executed on a system, it allows us to remotely control that system.

35
00:02:54,320 --> 00:02:57,740
So basically hack it and gain full control over it.

36
00:02:58,450 --> 00:03:04,030
Now, just like all the other tools, we're going to program this from scratch, and I'm going to show

37
00:03:04,030 --> 00:03:10,600
you how to write this in a generic way so that you can use the information you learn here in many scenarios.

38
00:03:10,600 --> 00:03:15,490
For example, you'll be able to write a web server, a chat program, and so on.

39
00:03:16,950 --> 00:03:17,850
Now, right here.

40
00:03:17,850 --> 00:03:19,680
I'm already at the hacker machine.

41
00:03:19,680 --> 00:03:24,390
And as you can see, it's already listening and waiting for incoming connections.

42
00:03:25,140 --> 00:03:30,480
Once the backdoor gets executed on a computer, we will get a connection.

43
00:03:30,480 --> 00:03:37,800
As you can see here, once we have the connection, we'll be able to use all the features implemented

44
00:03:37,800 --> 00:03:41,490
in this backdoor which we're going to implement ourselves.

45
00:03:41,970 --> 00:03:44,790
So first of all, I have file system access.

46
00:03:44,790 --> 00:03:49,980
So if I do the it will show me my current working directory.

47
00:03:49,980 --> 00:03:53,760
And as you can see, I'm in users Z downloads.

48
00:03:54,210 --> 00:03:58,590
That's basically because the backdoor is actually stored in this location.

49
00:03:59,560 --> 00:04:07,930
So I can do c d dot dot to go back one directory and if I do KD now to see where am I?

50
00:04:07,960 --> 00:04:14,110
You can see I'm in Z and we can also execute all system commands.

51
00:04:14,200 --> 00:04:18,480
And keep in mind this factor works on all operating systems.

52
00:04:18,490 --> 00:04:25,030
It works on Linux, Windows and OS X, so you can use the system commands of your target and they will

53
00:04:25,030 --> 00:04:26,320
work by default.

54
00:04:26,680 --> 00:04:28,720
So right now my target is Windows.

55
00:04:28,720 --> 00:04:32,320
So if I wanted to list the directories, I can do it there.

56
00:04:32,680 --> 00:04:38,770
And as you can see, I get a list of all the files and directories in the current working directory.

57
00:04:39,520 --> 00:04:44,020
Now I can go back in downloads by doing see the downloads.

58
00:04:45,200 --> 00:04:51,260
And if I list again in here, you'll see the files that we have in the downloads, which are the back

59
00:04:51,260 --> 00:04:55,490
door and the GT-R image which I have right here.

60
00:04:57,780 --> 00:05:03,360
Now I also want to highlight a feature that we're going to program, which is the ability to download

61
00:05:03,360 --> 00:05:04,170
files.

62
00:05:05,100 --> 00:05:11,610
So I'm just going to do a download and I'm going to follow it by the file name that I want to download

63
00:05:11,610 --> 00:05:12,720
and it's the image.

64
00:05:12,720 --> 00:05:15,810
So it's got all the jpg.

65
00:05:17,020 --> 00:05:21,160
And as you can see, I'll see it automatically being downloaded in here.

66
00:05:21,160 --> 00:05:26,680
And if we double click this, we have the image intact and we're able to open it.

67
00:05:27,850 --> 00:05:33,250
Now I'm downloading the image just as an example, but it just goes to show you that you'll be able

68
00:05:33,250 --> 00:05:36,520
to download any file from the target system.

69
00:05:37,600 --> 00:05:43,360
You can also upload files, which is really, really useful because you'll be able to upload evil files,

70
00:05:43,360 --> 00:05:45,820
viruses, keyloggers and so on.

71
00:05:46,060 --> 00:05:54,910
And as an example, I'm just going to rename this image to GTR two dot JPG and I'm going to upload it.

72
00:05:57,060 --> 00:06:02,580
So I'm going to do upload G2 or two dot jpg.

73
00:06:04,260 --> 00:06:11,160
Now it's tell me the upload is successful and if we go here again, you can see we have the new image.

74
00:06:12,040 --> 00:06:18,040
Now, if this was an evil file because you're able to execute system commands from the back door, you'll

75
00:06:18,040 --> 00:06:23,710
be able to just call it from here and execute it remotely on the target computer.

76
00:06:25,060 --> 00:06:29,470
The last example that I want to show you is a vulnerability scanner.

77
00:06:30,040 --> 00:06:36,790
This is a program that can automatically discover weaknesses in websites and web applications.

78
00:06:36,790 --> 00:06:40,690
So all you have to do is just give this program a website.

79
00:06:40,690 --> 00:06:47,480
And first of all, it's going to discover all the links and all the pages in the Target website.

80
00:06:47,500 --> 00:06:55,050
Then for each one of these pages, it's going to extract all the parameters and all the forms.

81
00:06:55,060 --> 00:07:03,190
And finally, for each one of these parameters and forms, it's going to submit code to discover vulnerabilities.

82
00:07:03,820 --> 00:07:07,660
Then once it's done, it's going to show me the results.

83
00:07:07,660 --> 00:07:14,710
And as you can see, it was able to discover an XSS vulnerability in here, just like all the other

84
00:07:14,710 --> 00:07:15,370
programs.

85
00:07:15,370 --> 00:07:18,960
We're going to write this from scratch ourselves.

86
00:07:18,970 --> 00:07:21,520
So again, don't worry about how this works.

87
00:07:21,520 --> 00:07:25,780
And don't worry if you don't understand what a vulnerability is and all of that.

88
00:07:25,780 --> 00:07:29,290
We will cover all of this later on in the course.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,810 --> 00:00:05,130
Now, as I promised you, this course is going to be highly practical.

2
00:00:05,130 --> 00:00:08,760
But I also promised that I will not neglect the theory.

3
00:00:09,210 --> 00:00:14,850
So I want to cover some terminology before we jump into hacking and programming.

4
00:00:15,750 --> 00:00:22,890
Now, what we mean by hacking is being able to gain access to a system that you're not supposed to have

5
00:00:22,890 --> 00:00:23,940
access to.

6
00:00:23,970 --> 00:00:30,510
So, for example, logging into an email account that you're not supposed to have access to is considered

7
00:00:30,540 --> 00:00:31,020
hacking.

8
00:00:31,020 --> 00:00:38,190
That account, gaining access to a remote computer that you're not supposed to have access to is hacking

9
00:00:38,220 --> 00:00:39,300
that computer.

10
00:00:39,390 --> 00:00:45,450
Reading information that you're not supposed to be able to read again is considered hacking.

11
00:00:46,170 --> 00:00:52,290
So you can see that there is a large number of ways to hack into a system, and the word hacking can

12
00:00:52,290 --> 00:00:54,060
refer to a number of things.

13
00:00:54,060 --> 00:01:00,750
But the main concept is the same gaining access or being able to do things that you're not supposed

14
00:01:00,750 --> 00:01:03,840
to be able to do is considered hacking.

15
00:01:04,380 --> 00:01:06,900
Now, there are three main types of hackers.

16
00:01:06,900 --> 00:01:08,670
We have the black hat hackers.

17
00:01:08,670 --> 00:01:15,900
These are the bad people that hack systems illegally in order to steal money or to achieve their own

18
00:01:15,900 --> 00:01:17,190
illegal goals.

19
00:01:17,910 --> 00:01:21,150
The second part of hackers are the white hat hackers.

20
00:01:21,150 --> 00:01:22,500
So this is us.

21
00:01:22,890 --> 00:01:29,550
These people use the same techniques used by the Blackhat hackers, and they also hack into systems,

22
00:01:29,550 --> 00:01:37,170
but they only hack into systems that they have permission to hack in order to test the security of these

23
00:01:37,170 --> 00:01:37,950
systems.

24
00:01:38,700 --> 00:01:42,110
And finally, we have the grey hat hackers.

25
00:01:42,120 --> 00:01:44,400
These are a mixture of the two.

26
00:01:44,400 --> 00:01:50,460
So they'll hack into any system, even if they don't have permission to test the security of the system.

27
00:01:50,610 --> 00:01:54,300
But they will never steal money or damage that system.

28
00:01:54,300 --> 00:01:59,400
And in most cases, they'll go ahead and tell the administrator of that system.

29
00:01:59,400 --> 00:02:06,840
But what they do is still illegal because they test the security of systems that they do not own or

30
00:02:06,840 --> 00:02:08,820
do not have permission to test.

31
00:02:09,570 --> 00:02:13,530
Now, one big question that people always ask is why hacking?

32
00:02:13,530 --> 00:02:15,390
Why teach or learn hacking?

33
00:02:15,420 --> 00:02:17,990
Well, hacking is an existing industry.

34
00:02:18,000 --> 00:02:23,550
There are lots of job opportunities for ethical hackers, for people like us who would go ahead and

35
00:02:23,550 --> 00:02:30,000
test the security of systems to make sure that these systems are secure and not vulnerable to blackhat

36
00:02:30,030 --> 00:02:30,710
hackers.

37
00:02:30,720 --> 00:02:37,260
There is actually a huge demand for ethical hackers these days because of the increased amount of hacking

38
00:02:37,260 --> 00:02:37,860
attacks.

39
00:02:37,860 --> 00:02:42,690
So we hear on the daily big systems and big companies are getting hacked.

40
00:02:42,900 --> 00:02:50,070
Not so long ago, Uber attacked and information of more than 56 million users were exposed.

41
00:02:50,070 --> 00:02:56,670
So companies like Uber, Facebook and Google, they hire hackers in order to try and hack into their

42
00:02:56,670 --> 00:03:00,930
systems and tell them where the weaknesses are so that they can fix it.

43
00:03:01,320 --> 00:03:07,200
They also have bug bounty programs where they literally ask all the hackers around the world to go ahead

44
00:03:07,200 --> 00:03:10,320
and try to hack into their websites and with their web applications.

45
00:03:10,320 --> 00:03:14,190
And if they could, they will pay them a bounty for the box that they find.

46
00:03:15,460 --> 00:03:17,430
Now, thus the hacking part covered.

47
00:03:17,440 --> 00:03:22,120
But this course is not only a hacking course, we will also cover programming.

48
00:03:22,870 --> 00:03:25,330
So what do we mean by programming?

49
00:03:25,750 --> 00:03:29,500
Well, programming is the process of writing a program.

50
00:03:29,920 --> 00:03:33,160
And this vague definition leads us to the next question.

51
00:03:33,160 --> 00:03:34,570
What is a program?

52
00:03:34,750 --> 00:03:40,660
Well, a program is a set of instructions that will allow us to solve a problem.

53
00:03:40,900 --> 00:03:47,650
For example, we have the Facebook app or the Facebook website, and the problem that it solves is social

54
00:03:47,650 --> 00:03:48,370
networking.

55
00:03:48,370 --> 00:03:51,640
So it allows us to stay connected with our friends and family.

56
00:03:52,390 --> 00:03:56,110
Another example is your media player such as iTunes.

57
00:03:56,140 --> 00:03:58,990
It solves the problem of playing music for you.

58
00:03:59,350 --> 00:04:04,420
So if you think of it, any program that you use solves a certain problem.

59
00:04:05,290 --> 00:04:10,690
So programming is the process of writing a program to solve a problem.

60
00:04:11,110 --> 00:04:18,400
Now, since this is a hacking and programming course, our problem is always going to be trying to hack

61
00:04:18,400 --> 00:04:19,990
into a certain system.

62
00:04:20,350 --> 00:04:26,860
So basically, we will be writing programs that will allow us to hack into networks, computers, websites

63
00:04:26,860 --> 00:04:27,820
and so on.

64
00:04:29,320 --> 00:04:30,040
Now.

65
00:04:30,040 --> 00:04:33,970
The solution for the problem cannot be written in plain English.

66
00:04:34,000 --> 00:04:38,020
You're going to have to write it in a language that the computer can understand.

67
00:04:38,080 --> 00:04:41,950
So you're going to have to use a programming language to do that.

68
00:04:42,910 --> 00:04:47,110
Now you can use any programming language to write programs for hacking.

69
00:04:47,200 --> 00:04:51,250
But in this course, we're only going to be using Python.

70
00:04:51,640 --> 00:04:56,950
The reason why I picked Python because it's a very simple language, yet powerful.

71
00:04:57,280 --> 00:05:04,150
It's open source and object oriented, and it has great libraries that can be used for both for hacking

72
00:05:04,150 --> 00:05:05,230
and for writing.

73
00:05:05,230 --> 00:05:09,010
Very useful normal programs other than hacking programs.

74
00:05:10,040 --> 00:05:15,320
Now, the last question that you might think we covered, why you'd want to learn hacking, but why

75
00:05:15,320 --> 00:05:17,030
would you want to learn programming?

76
00:05:17,240 --> 00:05:20,900
Well, programming is a very, very important skill these days.

77
00:05:20,930 --> 00:05:23,630
Everything around us is controlled by computers.

78
00:05:23,630 --> 00:05:27,590
And the only way to communicate with computers is to write programs.

79
00:05:27,620 --> 00:05:33,290
Some countries are even starting to teach programming to their school children because they think it

80
00:05:33,290 --> 00:05:37,010
is just as important as learning maths and other subjects.

81
00:05:37,670 --> 00:05:42,920
Now, since you want to be a hacker or you're interested in hacking, then programming is very, very

82
00:05:42,920 --> 00:05:49,040
important for you, especially because it will allow you to start implementing your own attacks.

83
00:05:49,040 --> 00:05:54,710
So instead of using other tools made by other people, you're going to be able to write your own tools

84
00:05:54,710 --> 00:06:00,380
to exploit weaknesses that you discover or to carry out attacks that nobody has ever implemented.

85
00:06:00,950 --> 00:06:06,770
Not only that, but you're going to have the ability to read programs written by other people and extend

86
00:06:06,770 --> 00:06:12,980
them, fix them if they have bugs, or extend them to make them fit your situation or your scenario.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,290 --> 00:00:04,610
As I mentioned, this course is going to be highly practical.

2
00:00:04,640 --> 00:00:11,720
Therefore, you will need a lab, a place where you can practice and experiment with the things that

3
00:00:11,720 --> 00:00:14,060
you're going to learn in the course.

4
00:00:14,780 --> 00:00:22,400
Not only that, but having a lab is essential for hackers or pentesters because let's say you discovered

5
00:00:22,400 --> 00:00:27,920
a new attack, or you want to experiment with a new attack, or you learn something new online and you

6
00:00:27,920 --> 00:00:34,700
want to test it, then the perfect place for you to test this and practice hacking is a hacking or a

7
00:00:34,700 --> 00:00:35,720
testing lab.

8
00:00:36,560 --> 00:00:42,650
Now, to create a lab, you just need a number of computers with different operating systems and you

9
00:00:42,650 --> 00:00:44,360
will need a number of networks.

10
00:00:44,360 --> 00:00:50,000
If you are testing network attacks and if you're testing website attacks, then you will need to install

11
00:00:50,000 --> 00:00:52,010
websites on these computers.

12
00:00:52,310 --> 00:01:01,460
Or you can do all of this inside your computer, inside one machine and install everything else as virtual

13
00:01:01,460 --> 00:01:02,420
machines.

14
00:01:02,420 --> 00:01:04,730
And this is what we're going to do.

15
00:01:04,730 --> 00:01:07,610
So let's say you have your computer right here.

16
00:01:07,610 --> 00:01:13,970
This is your main computer that you use every day to check your emails or even to watch this course.

17
00:01:14,530 --> 00:01:18,520
This computer might be running windows OS, X or Linux.

18
00:01:18,520 --> 00:01:19,690
It doesn't really matter.

19
00:01:19,690 --> 00:01:21,580
It can run any operating system.

20
00:01:21,580 --> 00:01:30,580
You can keep all the programs that you love and like and install a virtualization software such as VirtualBox

21
00:01:30,580 --> 00:01:31,870
or VMware.

22
00:01:31,900 --> 00:01:40,720
A virtualization software allows us to create a number of virtual computers inside your main computer,

23
00:01:40,720 --> 00:01:50,230
so we can have 3 or 4 or 5 or 10 computers, all installed inside your main operating system, inside

24
00:01:50,230 --> 00:01:51,520
your main computer.

25
00:01:52,460 --> 00:01:56,990
The really cool thing about this is you will not need a number of computers.

26
00:01:56,990 --> 00:02:00,950
You can do everything inside a single computer.

27
00:02:01,780 --> 00:02:05,590
So what we're going to do is we're going to have a hacking machine.

28
00:02:05,590 --> 00:02:11,170
This is going to be the machine that we will use in order to hack into other machines.

29
00:02:11,170 --> 00:02:16,480
We're going to install Kali Linux on it, and we'll talk about what that is later on.

30
00:02:17,020 --> 00:02:20,290
And we're going to have two target machines.

31
00:02:20,290 --> 00:02:25,750
These are machines that we're going to try to hack into and practice hacking against.

32
00:02:26,230 --> 00:02:31,990
One of them is going to be running windows, and the other one is going to be running an operating system

33
00:02:31,990 --> 00:02:33,430
called Metasploitable.

34
00:02:33,430 --> 00:02:36,010
And we will talk about that once we get to it.

35
00:02:37,260 --> 00:02:44,820
Now, it is very important to understand that you will not lose any functionality when you install an

36
00:02:44,820 --> 00:02:52,410
operating system as a virtual machine, you can use that machine exactly as if it's a real physical

37
00:02:52,410 --> 00:02:53,160
machine.

38
00:02:53,880 --> 00:02:59,580
So the really cool thing about using virtual machines, first of all, you're not going to lose any

39
00:02:59,580 --> 00:03:00,750
functionality.

40
00:03:00,780 --> 00:03:04,440
Second, all the machines are going to be isolated.

41
00:03:04,440 --> 00:03:09,060
So as we go through the course, we're going to be trying to hack into these machines.

42
00:03:09,060 --> 00:03:12,990
And we might actually break one of them, but that's fine.

43
00:03:12,990 --> 00:03:15,180
It will not affect your host machine.

44
00:03:15,180 --> 00:03:22,500
You will be able to easily fix this and go back to a snapshot of when the machine was working, without

45
00:03:22,500 --> 00:03:24,810
affecting your main computer.

46
00:03:25,590 --> 00:03:30,990
The other cool thing is that you'll be able to have a number of different versions of each of these

47
00:03:30,990 --> 00:03:35,670
machines, and you'll be able to change the configuration easily.

48
00:03:35,670 --> 00:03:42,330
Change the network settings, or switch between different states of these machines, and so on.

49
00:03:42,930 --> 00:03:51,180
So by using virtualization, you will not lose any functionality, but you will gain so many cool features.

50
00:03:51,720 --> 00:03:55,770
And the main thing to keep in mind is your host machine.

51
00:03:55,770 --> 00:03:58,500
Your main computer will never be affected.

52
00:03:58,500 --> 00:04:00,450
It's completely isolated.

53
00:04:00,450 --> 00:04:07,950
And if we break any of these machines, nothing will happen to your precious computer that you use every

54
00:04:07,950 --> 00:04:08,610
day.

55
00:04:08,610 --> 00:04:14,130
And this way you can always use your host machine for daily use, for checking emails, watching the

56
00:04:14,130 --> 00:04:20,790
videos, doing whatever you do normally, and then use your virtual machines for hacking and practicing

57
00:04:20,790 --> 00:04:21,690
the attacks.

58
00:04:22,290 --> 00:04:25,440
And this is what we're going to be doing in this course.

59
00:04:25,440 --> 00:04:31,260
So right here I have a windows computer that does not contain any hacking tools.

60
00:04:31,260 --> 00:04:33,480
So I can't really use it for hacking.

61
00:04:33,480 --> 00:04:39,240
But I have a virtualization software called VMware installed on it.

62
00:04:39,240 --> 00:04:49,470
And this software allows me to run multiple operating systems as virtual machines within this host computer,

63
00:04:49,860 --> 00:04:56,790
so I can use the host computer for my daily use, and then use the virtual machines for whatever use

64
00:04:56,790 --> 00:04:59,160
I want for testing or for hacking.

65
00:04:59,610 --> 00:05:04,920
So if I zoom in here on the left, you can see that I have a Kali Linux installed.

66
00:05:04,920 --> 00:05:07,980
This is an operating system that we will use for hacking.

67
00:05:07,980 --> 00:05:10,500
You can see I have another windows computer.

68
00:05:10,500 --> 00:05:14,790
You can see that I have Linux, all of it installed as virtual machines.

69
00:05:14,790 --> 00:05:20,820
And then we're going to be using these as we go through the course for hacking or for testing without

70
00:05:20,820 --> 00:05:25,890
affecting our main host operating system, this windows computer.

71
00:05:26,700 --> 00:05:29,280
Now don't worry about any of this.

72
00:05:29,280 --> 00:05:36,690
In the next lectures I will show you in details how to install this virtualization software VMware on

73
00:05:36,690 --> 00:05:43,140
all of the major operating systems on windows, Linux or Apple Mac OS.

74
00:05:43,740 --> 00:05:50,700
As we go through the course, I will also show you in details how to install these operating systems

75
00:05:50,700 --> 00:05:53,700
as virtual machines within VMware.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,110 --> 00:00:07,040
The first operating system that we're going to install in our lab is Kali Linux.

2
00:00:07,400 --> 00:00:12,170
See, throughout the course we're going to use a number of hacking tools.

3
00:00:12,470 --> 00:00:15,440
You can install each of these tools manually.

4
00:00:15,440 --> 00:00:22,820
Or you can do what most hackers do, including myself, and save time and effort and use an operating

5
00:00:22,820 --> 00:00:25,040
system designed for hacking.

6
00:00:25,640 --> 00:00:29,240
We're going to use an operating system called Kali Linux.

7
00:00:29,270 --> 00:00:36,980
It's a Linux distro based on Debian, and the only difference between Kali and the actual original Debian

8
00:00:36,980 --> 00:00:44,570
Linux distro is the fact that Kali has a lot of hacking and penetration testing tools, pre-installed

9
00:00:44,570 --> 00:00:46,490
and pre-configured in it.

10
00:00:46,520 --> 00:00:53,450
Therefore, once you install Kali, you will have access to so many hacking tools without the need to

11
00:00:53,450 --> 00:00:58,970
install or configure any of them, which will save you a lot of time and a lot of effort.

12
00:00:59,510 --> 00:01:06,380
Now, just like any other operating system, you can install it as a main machine or as a virtual machine.

13
00:01:06,860 --> 00:01:12,770
Now I'm going to install it as a virtual machine because like I explained in the previous lecture,

14
00:01:12,770 --> 00:01:18,200
we will not lose any functionality by using operating systems as virtual machines.

15
00:01:18,200 --> 00:01:24,680
And it comes with a lot of benefits because it's completely isolated from our computer that we always

16
00:01:24,680 --> 00:01:25,100
use.

17
00:01:25,100 --> 00:01:30,980
So if we break the virtual machine or if we want to delete it and reset it, it will not affect our

18
00:01:30,980 --> 00:01:32,570
main operating system.

19
00:01:33,430 --> 00:01:39,100
Now, the first thing that you want to do is download the virtual image of Kali Linux, so you can download

20
00:01:39,100 --> 00:01:41,410
it from the resources of this lecture.

21
00:01:41,770 --> 00:01:47,050
To access the resources of the current lecture, we're going to scroll down to the lecture.

22
00:01:47,050 --> 00:01:50,170
We're going to click on the resources icon.

23
00:01:50,170 --> 00:01:53,440
And then we're going to click on the resource that we want.

24
00:01:55,400 --> 00:02:02,450
Now, before going ahead with this, I want to highlight something that is very, very, very important.

25
00:02:02,450 --> 00:02:04,160
So please pay attention.

26
00:02:04,430 --> 00:02:09,500
This is a custom Kali image that I made for my courses.

27
00:02:09,500 --> 00:02:12,830
It is smoother and faster than the original one.

28
00:02:12,830 --> 00:02:20,480
It fixes a number of bugs, and it contains a number of extra programs that we need for this course

29
00:02:20,480 --> 00:02:24,230
and are not included in the original release.

30
00:02:25,000 --> 00:02:28,210
You do not have to use this custom image.

31
00:02:28,210 --> 00:02:30,550
You can use the original one.

32
00:02:30,550 --> 00:02:34,360
You can even use just a normal Linux distribution.

33
00:02:34,360 --> 00:02:42,400
As long as you know how to manually install all of the missing programs and apply the fixes that I did

34
00:02:42,400 --> 00:02:43,150
myself.

35
00:02:43,150 --> 00:02:46,600
And you can see a full list of them here on the left.

36
00:02:47,960 --> 00:02:54,590
Therefore, if you ask us a question in the Q&amp;A section and you're not using the custom image, the

37
00:02:54,590 --> 00:03:01,220
first thing that we will ask you is to use the custom Kali image that we made for you, because we will

38
00:03:01,220 --> 00:03:05,060
not know the changes you made in order to fix it.

39
00:03:05,540 --> 00:03:13,400
So if you have a 64 bit computer, download it from one of these links, or if you have a 32 bit computer,

40
00:03:13,400 --> 00:03:16,820
then download the 32 bit version from here.

41
00:03:17,410 --> 00:03:18,760
For Apple users.

42
00:03:18,760 --> 00:03:23,470
If you have an Apple computer, then you should download the 64 bit version.

43
00:03:23,470 --> 00:03:31,660
If you have an older Apple computer that uses a 64 bit processor, or if you have a newer Apple computer

44
00:03:31,660 --> 00:03:39,280
with a silicon ARM chip like the M1, M2, M3, and so on, then make sure you download the version

45
00:03:39,280 --> 00:03:42,970
that is made specifically for your computer from here.

46
00:03:44,110 --> 00:03:50,320
While waiting on the download, check out our VIP community right here and our YouTube channel.

47
00:03:50,320 --> 00:03:56,260
We actually post hacking content on it regularly, so you'll probably find this very, very useful.

48
00:03:56,260 --> 00:04:01,390
So make sure you subscribe to stay updated with the latest stuff that we post in here.

49
00:04:01,420 --> 00:04:07,870
Once the download is complete, you'll be ready to install Kali as a virtual machine.

50
00:04:07,870 --> 00:04:10,960
I will walk you through that in the next lectures.

51
00:04:10,960 --> 00:04:17,350
But before moving on, we need to enable virtualization to run virtual machines.

52
00:04:17,350 --> 00:04:21,430
You only need to do that if you are running Windows or Linux.

53
00:04:21,430 --> 00:04:29,230
You do not need to do this if you're running Apple Mac OS, as Apple computers come with virtualization

54
00:04:29,230 --> 00:04:30,970
enabled by default.

55
00:04:30,970 --> 00:04:35,530
So skip the rest of this lecture if you're using an Apple computer.

56
00:04:35,530 --> 00:04:41,110
If you're using Windows or Linux, then continue with me to see how to enable it.

57
00:04:41,110 --> 00:04:43,810
So on windows before enabling it.

58
00:04:43,810 --> 00:04:49,060
If you want to check if it is already enabled, you're going to have to go to the start menu.

59
00:04:49,600 --> 00:04:51,820
We're going to look for the task manager.

60
00:04:53,280 --> 00:04:54,780
I have it right here.

61
00:04:55,800 --> 00:04:58,170
I'm going to click on more details.

62
00:04:58,560 --> 00:05:00,990
I'm going to go on performance.

63
00:05:00,990 --> 00:05:08,490
And if you look in here beside virtualization, if it's saying enabled for you, then you can skip the

64
00:05:08,490 --> 00:05:11,250
rest of this lecture and move on to the next lecture.

65
00:05:11,250 --> 00:05:18,030
If it's saying disabled, then continue with this lecture to learn how to enable virtualization.

66
00:05:18,540 --> 00:05:22,920
So to do that we're going to have to first restart the computer.

67
00:05:22,920 --> 00:05:29,850
And as it's booting, as it's starting you're going to have to press a key combination, usually one

68
00:05:29,850 --> 00:05:33,780
of the F keys from your keyboard to enter the Bios settings.

69
00:05:33,780 --> 00:05:35,940
And from there we can enable it.

70
00:05:35,940 --> 00:05:37,740
So let me show you how to do it.

71
00:05:37,740 --> 00:05:42,390
Keep in mind you only need to do this if virtualization is disabled.

72
00:05:42,390 --> 00:05:47,490
If it's enabled, then you can simply skip this and continue with the rest of the course.

73
00:05:48,090 --> 00:05:54,090
Entering the bias settings varies depending on the computer that you have and depending on the motherboard.

74
00:05:54,090 --> 00:05:54,780
On the right.

75
00:05:54,780 --> 00:06:01,230
Here I have a table of the most common computers, along with the buttons that you need to press in

76
00:06:01,230 --> 00:06:03,570
order to enter the bias settings.

77
00:06:03,570 --> 00:06:10,500
So as soon as you turn on the computer or restart it, you'll need to press the button relevant to your

78
00:06:10,500 --> 00:06:14,310
computer in here in order to enter the bias settings.

79
00:06:14,310 --> 00:06:18,300
Once you're there, you want to make sure you enable virtualization.

80
00:06:18,300 --> 00:06:21,990
So let me show you how to do this on a real computer.

81
00:06:22,800 --> 00:06:25,080
This is a Lenovo computer.

82
00:06:25,080 --> 00:06:31,920
Therefore, based on the table that I just showed you, I will need to press F2 as soon as I turn on

83
00:06:31,920 --> 00:06:35,910
this computer in order to enter into its Bios settings.

84
00:06:36,420 --> 00:06:39,720
So first I'm going to hit the power button to turn it on.

85
00:06:39,720 --> 00:06:44,550
And as it's being turned on, I'm going to keep pressing F2 to enter the settings.

86
00:06:46,020 --> 00:06:46,860
And perfect.

87
00:06:46,860 --> 00:06:50,040
As you can see, I am in the bias settings right now.

88
00:06:50,070 --> 00:06:56,220
Now keep in mind, depending on your computer, the settings might look different than what I have in

89
00:06:56,220 --> 00:06:56,880
here.

90
00:06:57,030 --> 00:07:01,830
The main thing you need to keep in mind is to enable virtualization.

91
00:07:02,430 --> 00:07:07,830
This option can be found in a different window in any of these windows or tabs, but the main thing

92
00:07:07,830 --> 00:07:14,970
you want to keep in mind is to look for an option called virtualization, Vtx, Amd-v, or any of the

93
00:07:14,970 --> 00:07:17,310
options in here and enable it.

94
00:07:17,790 --> 00:07:22,920
So as you can see for me, the first window that I see is just an information window.

95
00:07:22,920 --> 00:07:25,530
It doesn't really allow me to modify anything.

96
00:07:25,890 --> 00:07:30,300
Now you can use the keyboard to navigate between these windows or these tabs.

97
00:07:30,300 --> 00:07:35,040
So I'm going to press the right arrow to go to the configuration window.

98
00:07:35,190 --> 00:07:41,220
And in here the only thing that I want to modify is Intel virtualization technology.

99
00:07:41,610 --> 00:07:45,690
So I'm going to use the down arrow to navigate to that.

100
00:07:45,690 --> 00:07:47,190
And I'm going to hit enter.

101
00:07:47,190 --> 00:07:52,650
To change the setting press down to set it to enabled and hit enter again to select it.

102
00:07:53,010 --> 00:07:57,660
Now if you're not sure about how to use this interface because like I said, you might actually get

103
00:07:57,660 --> 00:07:59,190
a different interface.

104
00:07:59,190 --> 00:08:04,920
Keep an eye on the instructions that you see at the bottom as they tell you exactly how to navigate

105
00:08:04,920 --> 00:08:06,480
and modify the settings.

106
00:08:07,010 --> 00:08:09,350
Now I'm done with the configuration window.

107
00:08:09,350 --> 00:08:13,580
I'm going to press the right arrow again to go into the security tab.

108
00:08:13,580 --> 00:08:14,990
We're going to go to the exit.

109
00:08:14,990 --> 00:08:17,900
So again I'm going to press the right arrow again.

110
00:08:18,350 --> 00:08:22,520
And I'm going to select the first option exit Saving Settings.

111
00:08:23,120 --> 00:08:25,250
It's going to ask me do I really want to do this.

112
00:08:25,250 --> 00:08:30,350
I'm going to say yes the computer will restart with virtualization enabled.

113
00:08:30,350 --> 00:08:36,440
So in the next lectures when we get to installing virtual machines, these virtual machines will run

114
00:08:36,440 --> 00:08:37,850
with no issues.

115
00:08:38,930 --> 00:08:40,850
If you done everything properly.

116
00:08:40,850 --> 00:08:43,640
Now, if you go again to the task manager.

117
00:08:44,740 --> 00:08:49,900
Go to performance, you should see that virtualization is enabled.

118
00:08:50,870 --> 00:08:56,120
If you couldn't do that, please ask us in the Q&amp;A section and we'll be happy to help you.

119
00:08:56,480 --> 00:09:03,740
Keep in mind that you only need to do this if you're running Windows or Linux as the host computer on

120
00:09:03,740 --> 00:09:05,000
your main computer.

121
00:09:05,800 --> 00:09:12,370
If you're running Apple Mac OS, then you do not need to do this and you should have skipped this part.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,290 --> 00:00:08,000
In this lecture, I'm going to show you how to install Kali Linux as a virtual machine inside windows.

2
00:00:08,510 --> 00:00:15,320
Skip this lecture if you're running Apple macOS or Linux, as I will cover that in the next lectures.

3
00:00:15,920 --> 00:00:18,770
Now I already have it downloaded in here.

4
00:00:18,770 --> 00:00:23,660
This is an archive that contains the actual virtual image of Kali.

5
00:00:23,660 --> 00:00:28,610
We did that to compress its size so that it doesn't take a long time to download.

6
00:00:28,610 --> 00:00:35,060
So now that we have it downloaded, we're going to need to install a program that can uncompress seven

7
00:00:35,060 --> 00:00:38,060
zip archives and show us the contents.

8
00:00:38,420 --> 00:00:42,890
To do that, we're going to go to Chrome and you want to go to seven zip.org.

9
00:00:42,890 --> 00:00:45,830
I will include that in the resources of this lecture.

10
00:00:45,830 --> 00:00:50,540
And you want to download the version that is compatible with your operating system.

11
00:00:50,540 --> 00:00:55,130
I have a 64 bit operating system, so I'm downloading 64 bit.

12
00:00:55,830 --> 00:01:00,270
And once the download is complete, we can simply click on it to install it.

13
00:01:00,300 --> 00:01:02,010
Give it permissions.

14
00:01:02,610 --> 00:01:04,470
And click on install.

15
00:01:04,500 --> 00:01:07,920
Once the install is complete, we're going to click on close.

16
00:01:07,920 --> 00:01:11,430
And we're going to go back on our downloads directory.

17
00:01:11,430 --> 00:01:17,550
And right now because we installed seven zip this program, we're going to be able to uncompress this

18
00:01:17,550 --> 00:01:20,940
archive and see the virtual image inside it.

19
00:01:21,210 --> 00:01:23,100
So I'm going to right click.

20
00:01:23,100 --> 00:01:26,370
And as you can see I have an entry called seven zip now.

21
00:01:26,370 --> 00:01:34,410
And if I hover over it and click on extract here, it will uncompress that image and reveal its contents.

22
00:01:34,680 --> 00:01:41,910
As you can see now we have a new directory and you can see the extension now is dot VMware VM.

23
00:01:41,910 --> 00:01:47,460
So as mentioned in the previous lecture, VMware is the software that we're going to use to run different

24
00:01:47,460 --> 00:01:49,980
operating systems as virtual machines.

25
00:01:49,980 --> 00:01:56,550
And this file right here is designed to run inside VMware as a virtual machine.

26
00:01:56,550 --> 00:02:01,650
So in order to run it we're going to have to go ahead and install VMware.

27
00:02:01,650 --> 00:02:06,390
Now you can download VMware from the same page where you downloaded the custom Kali.

28
00:02:06,390 --> 00:02:10,050
I'm going to include its link in the resources of this lecture as well.

29
00:02:10,050 --> 00:02:15,720
I already have it opened in here, and we're just going to scroll down past the Kali download links.

30
00:02:15,720 --> 00:02:21,960
You'll see we have a new section here that has the VMware player download links for all operating systems.

31
00:02:21,960 --> 00:02:25,020
Make sure you download the correct one for your operating system.

32
00:02:25,020 --> 00:02:28,170
We're covering installing Kali on Windows in this lecture.

33
00:02:28,170 --> 00:02:30,240
That's why we're going to download the windows one.

34
00:02:30,240 --> 00:02:35,370
And installing Kali for other operating systems is covered in the next lectures as mentioned.

35
00:02:35,370 --> 00:02:38,760
So we're going to click on this link in here to download it.

36
00:02:39,730 --> 00:02:43,120
And we're going to click on download in here again.

37
00:02:43,120 --> 00:02:46,090
And that's going to start the download process for us.

38
00:02:47,850 --> 00:02:53,640
Once the download is complete, you can simply view it in your downloads or click it here to run it.

39
00:02:54,270 --> 00:02:55,710
Give it permissions.

40
00:02:56,580 --> 00:03:01,140
And similar to installing any software on windows, you really don't need to do much.

41
00:03:01,140 --> 00:03:05,010
We're simply just going to click on next, next, next next.

42
00:03:05,010 --> 00:03:06,330
Accept the agreement.

43
00:03:06,360 --> 00:03:09,300
Next next next and let it install.

44
00:03:09,330 --> 00:03:10,950
The installation is complete.

45
00:03:10,950 --> 00:03:12,690
We're going to click on finish.

46
00:03:13,140 --> 00:03:22,830
And now we should be able to run this virtual machine file that we downloaded the VMware VM file using

47
00:03:22,830 --> 00:03:25,320
VMware Workstation Player.

48
00:03:25,870 --> 00:03:28,540
So we're going to double click it to start it.

49
00:03:28,540 --> 00:03:30,460
And I'm going to minimize this.

50
00:03:31,340 --> 00:03:34,160
We're going to click on Open Virtual Machine in here.

51
00:03:34,160 --> 00:03:38,240
Or you can go on the player file and open.

52
00:03:38,600 --> 00:03:45,290
We're going to navigate to the location where we downloaded the virtual image of Kali in my downloads.

53
00:03:45,290 --> 00:03:47,930
And as you can see we have it in here.

54
00:03:47,930 --> 00:03:51,440
Keep in mind it's the VMware VM file.

55
00:03:51,440 --> 00:03:54,440
So it ends with dot VMware VM.

56
00:03:54,530 --> 00:03:56,000
We're going to click on it.

57
00:03:56,000 --> 00:03:57,770
We're going to click on open.

58
00:03:57,770 --> 00:04:02,840
And inside it we have another file the only one file that we can select.

59
00:04:02,840 --> 00:04:05,750
We're going to select it and click on open.

60
00:04:05,810 --> 00:04:09,710
If you get no errors as you can see in here in my case that's it.

61
00:04:09,710 --> 00:04:12,890
The machine is now installed and ready to be used.

62
00:04:13,190 --> 00:04:19,880
Now before running it I'm going to click on Edit Virtual Machine Settings or right click and settings

63
00:04:19,880 --> 00:04:23,480
just to give you an idea of what the settings look like.

64
00:04:23,480 --> 00:04:29,990
So you feel more comfortable with VMware, because modifying virtual machine settings is always the

65
00:04:29,990 --> 00:04:34,040
same regardless of what type of virtual machine it is.

66
00:04:34,430 --> 00:04:40,670
So as you can see in this window, first of all, we can modify the amount of memory that the virtual

67
00:04:40,670 --> 00:04:41,570
machine has.

68
00:04:41,570 --> 00:04:45,620
And this machine is set to two gigabytes of memory by default.

69
00:04:45,620 --> 00:04:46,700
That is enough.

70
00:04:46,700 --> 00:04:50,300
But you can increase it up or down using this slider.

71
00:04:51,040 --> 00:04:55,210
You can also modify the amount of processors that the machine has.

72
00:04:55,240 --> 00:04:59,110
So as you can see, this one has two processors assigned to it.

73
00:04:59,110 --> 00:04:59,980
That is plenty.

74
00:05:00,010 --> 00:05:04,090
It's enough as you'll see, but you can give it more if you want to give it more.

75
00:05:04,210 --> 00:05:08,350
Another really important option is the network adapter.

76
00:05:08,350 --> 00:05:10,990
And as you can see this is set to Nat.

77
00:05:10,990 --> 00:05:18,490
And this setting will basically create a virtual network where my host machine is going to be the router

78
00:05:18,490 --> 00:05:19,690
for this network.

79
00:05:19,690 --> 00:05:25,570
And then all the virtual machines are going to be clients connected to this network.

80
00:05:25,570 --> 00:05:29,860
So they're going to get internet connection from my host machine.

81
00:05:30,070 --> 00:05:37,990
And at the same time, all of my virtual machines will be connected to the same virtual network.

82
00:05:38,050 --> 00:05:44,740
This is very handy because my virtual machines will be able to communicate with each other, and therefore

83
00:05:44,740 --> 00:05:50,410
will be able to use the Kali machine to hack into the others, will be able to test network attacks

84
00:05:50,410 --> 00:05:52,210
and do much more.

85
00:05:52,240 --> 00:05:57,550
So this setting will first allow my virtual machines to have internet connection, and it will also

86
00:05:57,550 --> 00:06:02,350
allow them to communicate with each other through this virtual Nat network.

87
00:06:02,860 --> 00:06:10,180
Now this virtual network will rely on the connection of your host computer, so it will not need any

88
00:06:10,180 --> 00:06:13,630
extra interfaces, it will not need any extra adapters.

89
00:06:13,630 --> 00:06:20,530
And as far as the virtual machines are concerned, they are connected to an Ethernet network, but in

90
00:06:20,530 --> 00:06:24,280
reality they are connected through the host machine.

91
00:06:25,030 --> 00:06:30,310
You can also modify the display settings in here if you have a bigger screen or a smaller screen.

92
00:06:30,310 --> 00:06:38,350
And I usually like to tick this box in here, the accelerate 3D graphics, and that's pretty much it.

93
00:06:38,500 --> 00:06:40,510
So we're going to click on okay.

94
00:06:40,510 --> 00:06:44,680
And like I said you actually don't need to modify any of these settings.

95
00:06:44,680 --> 00:06:49,510
I just wanted to show you what kind of settings we can set for these virtual machines.

96
00:06:49,510 --> 00:06:54,460
But once you're ready to run the virtual machine, we're just going to click on the green play button

97
00:06:54,460 --> 00:06:55,360
to start it.

98
00:06:55,360 --> 00:06:58,870
Now I'm going to click on inside and click enter to start Carly.

99
00:06:59,230 --> 00:07:01,180
And I know it's starting very small.

100
00:07:01,180 --> 00:07:03,850
This is because I have a high resolution screen.

101
00:07:03,850 --> 00:07:06,910
It's probably going to start in the normal size for you.

102
00:07:06,910 --> 00:07:08,770
I'm just going to zoom in for you.

103
00:07:08,770 --> 00:07:09,850
And perfect.

104
00:07:09,850 --> 00:07:11,980
We have the login screen here for Carly.

105
00:07:11,980 --> 00:07:18,460
So the username is r o o t and the password is t o o r.

106
00:07:18,460 --> 00:07:19,390
The reverse.

107
00:07:19,660 --> 00:07:22,930
We're going to hit enter and perfect.

108
00:07:22,930 --> 00:07:30,790
As you can see now we are running this Linux operating system as a virtual machine inside my windows

109
00:07:30,790 --> 00:07:32,860
operating system in here.

110
00:07:32,860 --> 00:07:38,200
And we're going to use this Linux operating system for hacking as we go through the course.

111
00:07:38,650 --> 00:07:42,160
Now, before we wrap it up, I want to go into full screen.

112
00:07:42,160 --> 00:07:46,570
So we're going to click on the player and we're going to click on full screen.

113
00:07:46,870 --> 00:07:50,200
And as you can see this will automatically go in full screen.

114
00:07:50,200 --> 00:07:56,020
So again I'm still using Carly within my host operating system which is windows.

115
00:07:56,140 --> 00:07:58,990
And in most cases Carly will look normal.

116
00:07:58,990 --> 00:08:05,080
But as you can see, in my case the icons are very small and the text here on the top is very small.

117
00:08:05,080 --> 00:08:08,380
And that's because I have a 4K display.

118
00:08:08,710 --> 00:08:12,250
So we're going to need to manually modify the display settings.

119
00:08:12,250 --> 00:08:16,300
And to do that we're going to right click go to settings.

120
00:08:18,100 --> 00:08:21,760
And we're gonna set the scale to 200.

121
00:08:22,470 --> 00:08:24,450
I'm going to click on apply.

122
00:08:25,590 --> 00:08:28,590
We're going to keep the changes and perfect now.

123
00:08:28,620 --> 00:08:30,270
Carly looks normal for me.

124
00:08:30,300 --> 00:08:31,650
You don't have to do this.

125
00:08:31,680 --> 00:08:33,510
If Carly looked normal from the start.

126
00:08:33,510 --> 00:08:37,440
Only if you have a high resolution screen, you might have to do this.

127
00:08:37,830 --> 00:08:44,310
Now, I know this operating system looks a little bit confusing and scary, but don't worry about it

128
00:08:44,310 --> 00:08:44,910
at all.

129
00:08:44,910 --> 00:08:49,740
I will teach you how to use it step by step as we go through the course.

130
00:08:50,010 --> 00:08:55,080
If you face any issues while installing Carly, check out the featured questions first.

131
00:08:55,080 --> 00:09:01,050
And if you don't see your answer, feel free to ask us in the Q&amp;A or in the forums, and we will respond

132
00:09:01,050 --> 00:09:04,410
to you within a maximum of 15 hours.

133
00:09:04,560 --> 00:09:11,340
And to turn off Carly, you can simply click here, go to Power Off and Power Off.

134
00:09:12,130 --> 00:09:16,180
Click on power off one more time and the machine will shut down.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,400 --> 00:00:07,480
In this lecture, I'm going to show you how to install Kali Linux as a virtual machine on Apple Mac

2
00:00:07,480 --> 00:00:08,200
OS.

3
00:00:09,660 --> 00:00:16,860
Skip this lecture if you already have Kali and VMware installed, or if you want to install it on Linux,

4
00:00:16,860 --> 00:00:20,400
I will cover installing it on Linux in the next lecture.

5
00:00:20,670 --> 00:00:26,190
The installation steps that I'm going to show you will work on all Apple computers, whether they use

6
00:00:26,190 --> 00:00:32,310
the older 64 bit processors or the new silicon chips, the M1, M2, M3 and so on.

7
00:00:32,310 --> 00:00:35,880
Just make sure you download the correct version from the download page.

8
00:00:35,880 --> 00:00:41,280
If you have an older 64 bit Apple computer, then download the 64 bit version.

9
00:00:41,280 --> 00:00:46,680
If you have one of the newer Apple Silicon computers, then make sure you download the version that

10
00:00:46,680 --> 00:00:49,140
is made for the Apple Silicon computers.

11
00:00:49,710 --> 00:00:55,980
I already have the correct version downloaded for my computer in my downloads in here, but as you can

12
00:00:55,980 --> 00:00:59,280
see, it is compressed inside a seven zip archive.

13
00:00:59,280 --> 00:01:03,300
We did this to reduce the size of the image so you can download it faster.

14
00:01:03,300 --> 00:01:06,780
So in order to use it, we have to decompress it first.

15
00:01:06,780 --> 00:01:10,500
And to do that you can simply double click the archive.

16
00:01:10,500 --> 00:01:14,010
As you can see now the archive is getting decompressed.

17
00:01:14,010 --> 00:01:15,300
You want to give it some time.

18
00:01:15,300 --> 00:01:17,100
This might take a minute or so.

19
00:01:17,100 --> 00:01:19,380
Once done, you're going to get a new file.

20
00:01:19,380 --> 00:01:20,280
As you can see.

21
00:01:20,280 --> 00:01:24,720
And this new file has a dot VMware VM extension.

22
00:01:24,720 --> 00:01:31,830
So this is the actual virtual image file that will allow us to run Kali Linux as a virtual machine.

23
00:01:31,830 --> 00:01:37,890
To do this, to run Kali Linux as a virtual machine inside our current operating system, we're going

24
00:01:37,890 --> 00:01:40,560
to have to install a virtualization software.

25
00:01:40,560 --> 00:01:43,920
And the one that we're going to use is called VMware.

26
00:01:44,010 --> 00:01:49,170
You can download VMware from the same page where you downloaded the custom Kali Linux.

27
00:01:49,170 --> 00:01:53,250
I'm going to include its link in the resources of this lecture as well.

28
00:01:53,370 --> 00:01:58,380
And we're simply going to scroll down past the Kali download links.

29
00:01:58,380 --> 00:02:03,990
You'll see a section here that allows you to download VMware for all operating systems.

30
00:02:03,990 --> 00:02:09,240
Now, since we're installing Kali on an Apple computer, make sure you download the version that is

31
00:02:09,240 --> 00:02:10,740
made for Apple computers.

32
00:02:10,740 --> 00:02:15,750
So we're going to click on this link and we're going to click on download one more time.

33
00:02:17,320 --> 00:02:24,040
If you get a message like this one saying unverified download blocked, click download file anyway.

34
00:02:24,190 --> 00:02:29,830
You're only getting this because you're downloading the file from here, but you can validate the integrity

35
00:02:29,830 --> 00:02:32,260
of the file using the signature in here.

36
00:02:32,590 --> 00:02:37,870
Once the download is complete, you're going to see VMware downloaded in your downloads directory in

37
00:02:37,870 --> 00:02:38,410
finder.

38
00:02:38,410 --> 00:02:41,320
And to install it we're simply going to double click it.

39
00:02:42,020 --> 00:02:48,530
And installing it is identical to installing any program on Apple Mac OS, so simply going to double

40
00:02:48,530 --> 00:02:49,250
click it.

41
00:02:50,060 --> 00:02:51,380
Click open.

42
00:02:53,060 --> 00:02:54,650
Enter the password.

43
00:02:56,350 --> 00:02:57,850
Agree to the terms.

44
00:02:58,180 --> 00:03:02,620
This program is free to use for personal use, which is what we're doing here.

45
00:03:02,620 --> 00:03:07,270
So you can simply use the product key that I'm using myself here in this video.

46
00:03:07,270 --> 00:03:10,240
I'm also going to include it in the resources of this lecture.

47
00:03:10,240 --> 00:03:13,240
And we're going to click on continue and done.

48
00:03:14,130 --> 00:03:18,960
Now you might be asked for permissions to offer extra features, so we're going to allow it.

49
00:03:18,960 --> 00:03:22,260
You can allow this by clicking on the System Preferences here.

50
00:03:23,300 --> 00:03:26,570
Click on the lock icon to edit the preferences.

51
00:03:26,570 --> 00:03:27,860
Put the password.

52
00:03:28,640 --> 00:03:31,010
And tick the box next to VMware.

53
00:03:31,610 --> 00:03:34,070
Once done, we're going to close the preferences.

54
00:03:34,070 --> 00:03:40,700
And as you can see, we have VMware Welcome screen right here, which will allow you to create or import

55
00:03:40,700 --> 00:03:42,140
a new virtual machine.

56
00:03:42,140 --> 00:03:46,760
I'm actually going to close this because I want to show you how to launch the program from scratch,

57
00:03:46,760 --> 00:03:49,910
and then how to import and add virtual machines to it.

58
00:03:50,060 --> 00:03:55,940
So to do that, simply we're going to go to all applications, and you can look for VMware in the search

59
00:03:55,940 --> 00:04:00,350
bar or simply just scroll through until you find it.

60
00:04:01,820 --> 00:04:06,920
One click will start the program and you'll see that we're back to the same welcome screen that allows

61
00:04:06,920 --> 00:04:09,800
us to create or import new virtual machines.

62
00:04:09,800 --> 00:04:14,870
But we actually want to use a ready virtual machine that I've already created for you.

63
00:04:14,870 --> 00:04:21,020
So all you have to do is simply go back to the downloads where you downloaded the custom Kali in finder,

64
00:04:21,020 --> 00:04:25,880
and this is the image that I downloaded and showed you at the start of the lecture.

65
00:04:25,880 --> 00:04:29,960
The whole process that I showed you so far should work on all Apple computers.

66
00:04:29,960 --> 00:04:34,010
You just want to make sure that you download the correct version for your computer.

67
00:04:34,010 --> 00:04:38,840
If you have a silicon based ARM processor, then make sure you download the ARM version.

68
00:04:38,840 --> 00:04:43,940
If you have an older 64 bit processor, then make sure you download the 64 bit version.

69
00:04:44,480 --> 00:04:49,820
So I have the correct version downloaded in here, and I'm simply going to double click it to run it

70
00:04:49,820 --> 00:04:56,120
in VMware now, because this is the first time I run this machine, it's giving me an option to upgrade

71
00:04:56,120 --> 00:04:56,330
it.

72
00:04:56,330 --> 00:05:01,250
You might not always get this depending on your settings, but if you get it, click on upgrade this

73
00:05:01,250 --> 00:05:04,940
will give you more features and more freedom with using the virtual machine.

74
00:05:04,940 --> 00:05:07,400
And we're going to say okay for this warning message.

75
00:05:07,400 --> 00:05:09,290
And we're going to say that we copied it.

76
00:05:09,290 --> 00:05:10,070
And perfect.

77
00:05:10,070 --> 00:05:14,630
Now this virtual machine is actually booting inside my own operating system.

78
00:05:14,870 --> 00:05:16,820
So now it's asking us to log in.

79
00:05:16,820 --> 00:05:22,580
The default username is root rot and the default password is Tor.

80
00:05:22,580 --> 00:05:23,960
Tor.

81
00:05:23,960 --> 00:05:25,880
I'm going to hit enter to log in.

82
00:05:26,570 --> 00:05:27,470
And perfect.

83
00:05:27,470 --> 00:05:34,700
Now I'm logged in and I'm running Kali Linux as a virtual machine inside my main operating system,

84
00:05:34,700 --> 00:05:37,760
which is this Mac OS computer.

85
00:05:38,150 --> 00:05:44,330
To go on full screen, you can simply click the green button in here and the resolution will automatically

86
00:05:44,330 --> 00:05:45,170
change.

87
00:05:45,170 --> 00:05:49,310
I know this might look a little bit scary, but don't worry about it.

88
00:05:49,310 --> 00:05:54,800
I will walk you through it step by step as we go through the course, and you'll see how easy it is

89
00:05:54,800 --> 00:05:56,090
to use it for hacking.

90
00:05:57,180 --> 00:05:58,050
For now.

91
00:05:58,050 --> 00:06:03,090
To exit the full screen, you can simply go to the top, keep the mouse there and click the green button

92
00:06:03,090 --> 00:06:03,690
again.

93
00:06:04,380 --> 00:06:09,810
And to turn this machine off, you can simply click the turn off button in here, click the power button

94
00:06:09,810 --> 00:06:12,000
again and click Power Off.

95
00:06:13,930 --> 00:06:17,830
Click power off one more time and now the machine is off.

96
00:06:18,740 --> 00:06:23,120
Now in here on the top right, you can see we have a VMware icon.

97
00:06:23,120 --> 00:06:28,760
And if you click it, you'll see a list of all of the virtual machines installed inside your current

98
00:06:28,760 --> 00:06:29,810
operating system.

99
00:06:29,810 --> 00:06:33,980
As you can see, I only have one because we already installed one so far.

100
00:06:34,370 --> 00:06:39,140
If you click the Virtual Machine library in here again, it will show you a nice window with a list

101
00:06:39,140 --> 00:06:41,000
of all the installed virtual machines.

102
00:06:41,000 --> 00:06:46,730
And if you want to edit the settings of any of these machines, you can simply right click it and click

103
00:06:46,730 --> 00:06:47,540
on settings.

104
00:06:48,450 --> 00:06:53,940
This machine is already configured to run well, but you can modify this if you have more resources

105
00:06:53,940 --> 00:06:55,890
to get it to run even faster.

106
00:06:55,890 --> 00:07:01,170
So I just want to show you how to modify the settings, because the process is the same for any virtual

107
00:07:01,170 --> 00:07:03,900
machine that you might install, even in the future.

108
00:07:03,900 --> 00:07:08,460
Usually the main settings that affect performance are the processors and memory.

109
00:07:08,980 --> 00:07:14,470
So here you can adjust the amount of memory that this virtual machine can use by moving the slider.

110
00:07:15,130 --> 00:07:20,860
And you can change the number of processors that this machine can use using this drop down menu.

111
00:07:21,250 --> 00:07:24,790
By default, this is set to two processors and two gigabytes of memory.

112
00:07:24,790 --> 00:07:30,040
This is enough to run the machine decently, but if you have more resources, then feel free to increase

113
00:07:30,040 --> 00:07:32,980
this to get the virtual machine to run even faster.

114
00:07:33,340 --> 00:07:34,390
Now let's go back.

115
00:07:34,390 --> 00:07:38,800
And I also want to show you the network settings for this specific virtual machine.

116
00:07:38,800 --> 00:07:42,430
And as you can see by default this is set to share with my Mac.

117
00:07:42,430 --> 00:07:49,690
And this basically sets up a virtual network where my host machine is going to be the router for this

118
00:07:49,690 --> 00:07:56,230
network, and then all the virtual machines are going to be clients connected to this network.

119
00:07:56,230 --> 00:08:00,520
So they're going to get internet connection from my host machine.

120
00:08:00,760 --> 00:08:08,680
And at the same time, all of my virtual machines will be connected to the same virtual network.

121
00:08:08,740 --> 00:08:15,400
This is very handy because my virtual machines will be able to communicate with each other, and therefore

122
00:08:15,400 --> 00:08:21,070
will be able to use the Kali machine to hack into the others, will be able to test network attacks

123
00:08:21,070 --> 00:08:22,780
and do much more.

124
00:08:22,960 --> 00:08:28,210
So this setting will first allow my virtual machines to have internet connection, and it will also

125
00:08:28,210 --> 00:08:32,980
allow them to communicate with each other through this virtual Nat network.

126
00:08:33,550 --> 00:08:40,840
Now, this virtual network will rely on the connection of your host computer, so it will not need any

127
00:08:40,840 --> 00:08:44,290
extra interfaces, it will not need any extra adapters.

128
00:08:44,290 --> 00:08:51,190
And as far as the virtual machines are concerned, they are connected to an Ethernet network, but in

129
00:08:51,190 --> 00:08:54,940
reality they are connected through the host machine.

130
00:08:56,070 --> 00:08:56,760
Again.

131
00:08:56,760 --> 00:08:59,670
Click on Show All to go back to all of the other settings.

132
00:09:00,060 --> 00:09:02,790
But like I said, you don't need to modify anything.

133
00:09:02,790 --> 00:09:07,740
Everything should be configured and set properly for you to run with no issues at all.

134
00:09:07,980 --> 00:09:12,630
If you face any issues then check out the featured questions and feel free to ask us.

135
00:09:12,630 --> 00:09:16,230
We will respond to you within less than 15 hours.

136
00:09:16,890 --> 00:09:19,980
And like I said, don't worry about how to use Kali.

137
00:09:19,980 --> 00:09:24,210
I will cover that in details step by step as we go through the course.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,020 --> 00:00:07,010
In this lecture, I'm going to show you how to install Kali Linux as a virtual machine inside Linux.

2
00:00:07,830 --> 00:00:14,580
Skip this lecture if you have it already installed, and revise the previous lectures to see how to

3
00:00:14,580 --> 00:00:18,180
install it on Windows or Apple Mac OS.

4
00:00:18,790 --> 00:00:22,720
Now I already have it downloaded in here in my downloads.

5
00:00:22,720 --> 00:00:27,760
And as you can see, this is an archive with A7Z extension.

6
00:00:28,000 --> 00:00:32,560
The actual virtual image is compressed inside this archive.

7
00:00:32,560 --> 00:00:36,310
We did this to reduce its size so you can download it quicker.

8
00:00:36,310 --> 00:00:41,830
But in order to use this virtual image, we're going to have to uncompress the archive.

9
00:00:41,830 --> 00:00:46,330
To do that, you simply have to right click it and click on extract.

10
00:00:46,330 --> 00:00:46,870
Here.

11
00:00:47,410 --> 00:00:53,740
You're going to need to give it some time to fully uncompress the archive and reveal its content.

12
00:00:54,220 --> 00:01:00,640
Once this is done, you're going to see a new directory in here that contains the actual image.

13
00:01:00,640 --> 00:01:05,770
And you'll notice that this folder ends with dot VMware VM.

14
00:01:05,770 --> 00:01:12,670
So in order to run this virtual image of Kali Linux, we need to install a virtualization software.

15
00:01:12,670 --> 00:01:16,390
And the software that we're going to use is called VMware.

16
00:01:16,420 --> 00:01:21,010
Now you can download VMware from the same page where you downloaded the custom Kali.

17
00:01:21,010 --> 00:01:24,520
I'm going to include its link in the resources of this lecture as well.

18
00:01:24,520 --> 00:01:28,090
And we're just going to scroll down past the Kali download links.

19
00:01:28,090 --> 00:01:34,510
You'll see we have a new section here that has the VMware player download links for all operating systems.

20
00:01:34,510 --> 00:01:40,060
And since we're installing Kali on a Linux computer in this lecture, we're going to download the Linux

21
00:01:40,060 --> 00:01:42,760
version in here by clicking on this link.

22
00:01:43,000 --> 00:01:47,230
Click on download one more time and give it some time to download.

23
00:01:47,880 --> 00:01:52,680
Once the download is complete, we're going to be able to see it here in our downloads.

24
00:01:52,710 --> 00:01:59,310
This is the installer for VMware, the software that we're going to use to run virtual machines inside

25
00:01:59,310 --> 00:02:01,170
our current operating system.

26
00:02:01,680 --> 00:02:07,800
And to install it, we're first going to need to open our terminal and navigate to the location where

27
00:02:07,800 --> 00:02:11,190
we have this installer downloaded, which is in my downloads.

28
00:02:11,190 --> 00:02:15,960
So I'm going to use the CD command to change my working directory to downloads.

29
00:02:16,590 --> 00:02:19,560
And if I do ls to list all of the files.

30
00:02:19,560 --> 00:02:21,990
You'll see we have the file in here.

31
00:02:21,990 --> 00:02:27,810
And before running this file, we need to change its permissions to an executable.

32
00:02:27,900 --> 00:02:34,230
So to do that we're going to do chmod plus x followed by the file name.

33
00:02:34,920 --> 00:02:36,420
I'm going to hit enter.

34
00:02:36,420 --> 00:02:45,150
And now if I do ls you'll notice that the file name is listed in green meaning that this is an executable.

35
00:02:45,450 --> 00:02:52,620
Now before running this executable we're going to have to install the Build Essentials package because

36
00:02:52,620 --> 00:02:57,420
it is required by this installer to install VMware for us.

37
00:02:57,960 --> 00:03:05,370
Now I'm doing all of these steps on ubuntu, but installing VMware should be pretty much the same on

38
00:03:05,370 --> 00:03:09,420
most Linux and especially Debian based distributions.

39
00:03:09,870 --> 00:03:16,410
So in order to install the build essentials, I'm first going to have to update the list of sources

40
00:03:16,410 --> 00:03:18,540
that I can download packages from.

41
00:03:18,540 --> 00:03:23,880
And to do that we're going to do sudo apt get update.

42
00:03:24,360 --> 00:03:26,280
I'm going to input my password.

43
00:03:26,280 --> 00:03:30,960
Once the sources are updated we're ready to install the build essentials.

44
00:03:30,960 --> 00:03:39,720
So we're going to do sudo apt get install build essential gcc 12.

45
00:03:39,720 --> 00:03:43,260
We're going to hit enter I've already installed this.

46
00:03:43,260 --> 00:03:46,230
That's why it's automatically installing it for me.

47
00:03:46,230 --> 00:03:48,960
But in your case it's going to ask you to confirm.

48
00:03:48,960 --> 00:03:53,310
And all you'll have to do is simply hit enter to confirm that you want to install it.

49
00:03:53,610 --> 00:03:57,900
If you don't see any errors at all, it means everything got installed properly.

50
00:03:57,900 --> 00:04:03,030
So I'm going to clear the screen and I'm going to do LZ one more time just to show you what we want

51
00:04:03,030 --> 00:04:03,450
to run.

52
00:04:03,450 --> 00:04:07,710
We want to run this executable, which is the same file as this one.

53
00:04:07,710 --> 00:04:15,750
And to run executables in Linux you simply have to do dot forward slash followed by the executable name.

54
00:04:16,410 --> 00:04:16,920
Now.

55
00:04:16,920 --> 00:04:19,050
Also we need to run this as admin.

56
00:04:19,050 --> 00:04:22,890
Therefore I'm going to type sudo before the command.

57
00:04:23,580 --> 00:04:25,110
I'm going to hit enter.

58
00:04:25,650 --> 00:04:29,490
And we're going to give it some time to extract the installer.

59
00:04:29,490 --> 00:04:33,270
And once it's done we're going to go to all of our software.

60
00:04:33,270 --> 00:04:35,760
And we're going to run VMware player.

61
00:04:36,800 --> 00:04:39,980
We're going to accept the agreement and click next.

62
00:04:39,980 --> 00:04:41,480
Accept the license.

63
00:04:41,510 --> 00:04:47,750
Next we're going to say we don't want to share data and we click on finish.

64
00:04:47,780 --> 00:04:49,760
We're going to put the password.

65
00:04:51,000 --> 00:04:51,870
And perfect.

66
00:04:51,870 --> 00:04:52,740
Here we go.

67
00:04:53,220 --> 00:04:59,010
During the installation, you might also get an option to either use the free or the paid version.

68
00:04:59,010 --> 00:05:03,870
Make sure you select the free one because we're using it for non-commercial use.

69
00:05:03,870 --> 00:05:10,230
So right now we're ready to create or open ready virtual machines using VMware.

70
00:05:10,230 --> 00:05:16,290
And the virtual machine that we actually want to run is the customized version of Kali that we just

71
00:05:16,290 --> 00:05:18,570
downloaded at the start of the video.

72
00:05:19,020 --> 00:05:25,530
So to do that, we're simply going to click on open a Virtual machine or go to File and Open Virtual

73
00:05:25,530 --> 00:05:26,220
Machine.

74
00:05:26,220 --> 00:05:30,150
We're going to navigate to the location where we downloaded this virtual machine.

75
00:05:30,150 --> 00:05:32,010
So we're going to go to downloads.

76
00:05:32,010 --> 00:05:39,000
We're going to double click it in here and select the only file that we can select which is the VM file.

77
00:05:39,000 --> 00:05:40,770
We're going to double click it.

78
00:05:41,160 --> 00:05:42,180
And here we go.

79
00:05:42,180 --> 00:05:44,640
We have it imported in here in VMware.

80
00:05:44,640 --> 00:05:49,050
And we can easily start it now by clicking on Power on in here.

81
00:05:49,610 --> 00:05:55,160
Now, before doing that, I just want to show you how to modify the virtual machine settings.

82
00:05:55,160 --> 00:05:57,170
You do not need to do this.

83
00:05:57,170 --> 00:05:59,270
I'm simply showing you how to do that.

84
00:05:59,270 --> 00:06:02,540
So you get an idea on how to modify the settings.

85
00:06:02,540 --> 00:06:09,320
Because modifying virtual machine settings is always the same regardless of what type of virtual machine

86
00:06:09,320 --> 00:06:10,040
it is.

87
00:06:10,370 --> 00:06:15,380
So we're going to click on Edit Virtual Machine Settings and here to modify the settings.

88
00:06:15,380 --> 00:06:20,990
And as you can see the first thing that you can modify is the amount of memory that the virtual machine

89
00:06:20,990 --> 00:06:21,440
has.

90
00:06:21,440 --> 00:06:25,190
And you can increase it or decrease it by moving the slider.

91
00:06:25,550 --> 00:06:27,770
It's set to two gigabytes by default.

92
00:06:27,770 --> 00:06:28,730
And that is good.

93
00:06:28,730 --> 00:06:30,380
So I'm going to keep it at that.

94
00:06:30,620 --> 00:06:34,520
Next you can modify the number of processors that the machine has.

95
00:06:34,520 --> 00:06:36,980
Again it's set to two by default.

96
00:06:36,980 --> 00:06:40,190
I'm going to keep it at that because that's pretty good.

97
00:06:40,370 --> 00:06:42,950
You can also modify the network settings.

98
00:06:42,950 --> 00:06:47,720
And as you can see right now it's set to use a not network.

99
00:06:47,720 --> 00:06:50,180
We're also going to keep this as Nat.

100
00:06:50,180 --> 00:06:57,110
This is great because this setting will basically create a virtual network where my host machine is

101
00:06:57,110 --> 00:07:03,620
going to be the router for this network, and then all the virtual machines are going to be clients

102
00:07:03,620 --> 00:07:05,660
connected to this network.

103
00:07:05,660 --> 00:07:09,950
So they're going to get internet connection from my host machine.

104
00:07:10,190 --> 00:07:18,110
And at the same time, all of my virtual machines will be connected to the same virtual network.

105
00:07:18,110 --> 00:07:24,860
This is very handy because my virtual machines will be able to communicate with each other, and therefore

106
00:07:24,860 --> 00:07:30,500
will be able to use the Kali machine to hack into the others, will be able to test network attacks

107
00:07:30,500 --> 00:07:32,300
and do much more.

108
00:07:32,330 --> 00:07:37,670
So this setting will first allow my virtual machines to have internet connection, and it will also

109
00:07:37,670 --> 00:07:42,440
allow them to communicate with each other through this virtual Nat network.

110
00:07:43,010 --> 00:07:50,270
Now, this virtual network will rely on the connection of your host computer, so it will not need any

111
00:07:50,270 --> 00:07:53,750
extra interfaces, it will not need any extra adapters.

112
00:07:53,750 --> 00:08:00,650
And as far as the virtual machines are concerned, they are connected to an Ethernet network, but in

113
00:08:00,650 --> 00:08:04,370
reality they are connected through the host machine.

114
00:08:05,070 --> 00:08:11,310
Finally, you can also modify the display settings in here, and I usually like to tick this option

115
00:08:11,310 --> 00:08:13,680
to accelerate 3D graphics.

116
00:08:13,680 --> 00:08:19,740
If you get issues with it, you can untick it, but usually it gives you nicer and smoother animations

117
00:08:19,740 --> 00:08:21,180
with virtual machines.

118
00:08:21,840 --> 00:08:24,210
So we're going to save the settings.

119
00:08:24,210 --> 00:08:31,140
And we can go ahead and start this virtual machine by selecting it and clicking on Power on in here.

120
00:08:32,330 --> 00:08:37,190
We're going to say, I copied it and we're just going to give it some time to load.

121
00:08:38,210 --> 00:08:44,900
And as you can see, if everything goes as expected, you should get the default login screen for Kali.

122
00:08:45,080 --> 00:08:50,540
So I'm going to click in here and I'm going to put the username as root rot.

123
00:08:50,840 --> 00:08:54,050
Hit enter and the password is the reverse of that.

124
00:08:54,050 --> 00:08:56,270
So it's t o o r.

125
00:08:56,990 --> 00:09:00,050
We're going to hit enter and give it some time.

126
00:09:00,590 --> 00:09:02,240
And great as you can see.

127
00:09:02,240 --> 00:09:10,370
Now we have another operating system, Kali Linux, installed as a virtual machine inside our own main

128
00:09:10,370 --> 00:09:12,140
host operating system.

129
00:09:12,710 --> 00:09:19,520
Now we can put this in full screen by going to Virtual machine in here and clicking on enter full screen.

130
00:09:20,260 --> 00:09:26,770
This screen should automatically resize, as you can see in here, and in most cases the icons should

131
00:09:26,770 --> 00:09:32,320
look normal, but in my case they look small because I have a 4K display.

132
00:09:32,710 --> 00:09:36,250
So we're going to need to manually modify the display settings.

133
00:09:36,250 --> 00:09:40,240
And to do that we're going to right click go to settings.

134
00:09:41,840 --> 00:09:45,500
And we're gonna set the scale to 200.

135
00:09:46,270 --> 00:09:48,340
I'm going to click on apply.

136
00:09:49,370 --> 00:09:52,430
We're going to keep the changes and perfect now.

137
00:09:52,460 --> 00:09:54,080
Carly looks normal for me.

138
00:09:54,110 --> 00:09:55,490
You don't have to do this.

139
00:09:55,520 --> 00:09:57,350
If Carly looked normal from the start.

140
00:09:57,350 --> 00:10:01,220
Only if you have a high resolution screen, you might have to do this.

141
00:10:01,640 --> 00:10:08,780
Now, I know this operating system might look a little bit confusing and scary, but don't worry about

142
00:10:08,780 --> 00:10:09,470
it at all.

143
00:10:09,470 --> 00:10:09,860
We.

144
00:10:09,860 --> 00:10:14,540
I will teach you how to use it step by step as we go through the course.

145
00:10:14,840 --> 00:10:19,940
If you face any issues while installing Carly, check out the featured questions first.

146
00:10:19,940 --> 00:10:25,910
And if you don't see your answer, feel free to ask us in the Q&amp;A or in the forums, and we will respond

147
00:10:25,910 --> 00:10:29,210
to you within a maximum of 15 hours.

148
00:10:29,960 --> 00:10:36,710
And to turn off Carly, you can simply click here, go to Power off and Power Off.

149
00:10:37,470 --> 00:10:41,490
Click on Power-off one more time and the machine will shut down.



━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,200 --> 00:00:06,350
Now that we have Kali Linux installed, I want to give you a basic overview of this operating system

2
00:00:06,350 --> 00:00:09,050
in this lecture so that you're comfortable with it.

3
00:00:09,050 --> 00:00:11,810
And we're going to be using it throughout the whole course.

4
00:00:11,810 --> 00:00:16,100
So everything is going to become easier for you as we go through the course.

5
00:00:17,010 --> 00:00:20,970
Now, the first thing that I want to talk about is the status bar in here.

6
00:00:20,970 --> 00:00:24,630
So you can see we have an applications menu in here.

7
00:00:24,630 --> 00:00:29,400
And inside it you can see all the applications are categorized.

8
00:00:29,940 --> 00:00:37,200
So we have information gathering applications vulnerability analysis, web application analysis reverse

9
00:00:37,200 --> 00:00:39,330
engineering and so on.

10
00:00:39,900 --> 00:00:45,270
So you can go on each one of these entries and just select any program that you want to run.

11
00:00:45,900 --> 00:00:52,890
And the next menu that we have is the places, which shows you the most common places in the file system.

12
00:00:52,890 --> 00:00:56,520
So we have home, we have the documents downloads and so on.

13
00:00:57,240 --> 00:00:59,460
So you can access the places from here.

14
00:00:59,460 --> 00:01:01,020
And then you click on home.

15
00:01:01,020 --> 00:01:04,680
Or you can just click on the files icon in here on the left.

16
00:01:04,680 --> 00:01:10,650
And both of these will bring you to the home directory which is actually the root directory.

17
00:01:10,650 --> 00:01:13,020
So it says home in here.

18
00:01:13,020 --> 00:01:19,860
