━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:01,570 --> 00:00:11,590
So so far our program takes a URL as an input and it extracts all the forms in that URL and from each

2
00:00:11,590 --> 00:00:19,930
form it extracts the action, which is the URL that the form should be submitted to the type of HTTP

3
00:00:19,930 --> 00:00:27,310
method that should be used in order to submit this form and the names of all the inputs.

4
00:00:28,050 --> 00:00:37,140
So as we've seen before in the guest login info program, all we need in order to submit a form is the

5
00:00:37,140 --> 00:00:41,280
action URL and the names of the inputs.

6
00:00:41,940 --> 00:00:48,750
So first of all, if you look at the action URL in here, which we extracted from here.

7
00:00:50,300 --> 00:00:59,690
You'll see that this is a relative URL, so it will only work from this page or pages on this specific

8
00:00:59,690 --> 00:01:00,620
website.

9
00:01:00,650 --> 00:01:08,390
But if we make the request from our python code, we need to use the full URL with the HTTP with the

10
00:01:08,390 --> 00:01:12,270
website name or the IP and using it like this.

11
00:01:12,290 --> 00:01:13,520
It won't work.

12
00:01:14,510 --> 00:01:21,060
Now, depending on the way the web page is programmed, the action URL could be a full URL.

13
00:01:21,080 --> 00:01:25,570
It can be a relatively URL like so without a forward slash.

14
00:01:25,580 --> 00:01:33,500
So it means that the index dot PHP is in the current working directory and it can start with a forward

15
00:01:33,500 --> 00:01:38,120
slash, which means that index dot php is at the web root.

16
00:01:38,120 --> 00:01:40,700
So right here before the utility.

17
00:01:41,870 --> 00:01:42,710
Now.

18
00:01:42,890 --> 00:01:46,400
Luckily, you don't need to account for all of this.

19
00:01:46,490 --> 00:01:50,640
We can use a library in Python called URL paths.

20
00:01:50,660 --> 00:01:52,940
So I'm going to import it in here.

21
00:01:54,490 --> 00:02:05,050
And then what we're going to do is I'm going to say my post, your URL is equal to your URL pass, which

22
00:02:05,050 --> 00:02:09,130
is the library that I just imported the URL join.

23
00:02:10,560 --> 00:02:15,450
And what this function allows us to do is join two URLs.

24
00:02:15,450 --> 00:02:23,400
One is the full URL, so the target URL and the next is going to be the action.

25
00:02:23,400 --> 00:02:25,920
So it's only part of the URL.

26
00:02:26,490 --> 00:02:35,100
Now the result of this is going to be a proper full URL that starts with the domain name and contains

27
00:02:35,100 --> 00:02:36,510
the proper path.

28
00:02:37,350 --> 00:02:39,960
So if I print the post URL.

29
00:02:41,720 --> 00:02:43,550
And run the code.

30
00:02:43,790 --> 00:02:44,540
You'll see.

31
00:02:44,540 --> 00:02:50,600
Now I have the full URL which I should post the form to.

32
00:02:51,080 --> 00:02:58,950
You can actually implement code to remove the last part of the target URL and append the action to it.

33
00:02:58,970 --> 00:03:04,580
But like I said, depending on the way the page is programmed, you're going to have to add checks to

34
00:03:04,580 --> 00:03:11,720
check if the action already contains a full URL or if the action starts with a forward slash, or if

35
00:03:11,720 --> 00:03:13,790
it does not start with a forward slash.

36
00:03:14,000 --> 00:03:21,710
So an easier way is just to use the URL, pass that URL, join, and you give the full URL followed

37
00:03:21,710 --> 00:03:28,580
by the action and it will automatically join it for you and give you a URL that you can use in your

38
00:03:28,580 --> 00:03:29,510
post method.

39
00:03:30,670 --> 00:03:31,700
So that's done.

40
00:03:31,720 --> 00:03:35,440
That's the first part that we need in order to submit the form.

41
00:03:35,680 --> 00:03:45,130
The next part is to create a dictionary similar to this one right here in which each key is an input

42
00:03:45,130 --> 00:03:45,610
name.

43
00:03:45,610 --> 00:03:54,700
And we already extracted the input names and each value is a value for the relevant input.

44
00:03:55,390 --> 00:03:57,920
So we can do this very easily.

45
00:03:57,940 --> 00:04:02,320
First of all, I'm going to remove this print and I'm going to remove this print.

46
00:04:03,360 --> 00:04:04,440
On this print.

47
00:04:05,070 --> 00:04:11,910
And right here in this loop, we're looping over all the inputs and we're executing this code for each

48
00:04:11,910 --> 00:04:12,600
input.

49
00:04:13,170 --> 00:04:16,560
So the first thing we're doing is we're getting the input name.

50
00:04:17,130 --> 00:04:22,530
And I also want to use the same idea to get the input type.

51
00:04:23,500 --> 00:04:28,540
And this is going to be equal to input that get.

52
00:04:29,140 --> 00:04:32,050
And I want to get the type.

53
00:04:33,080 --> 00:04:41,300
Now, the reason why I'm doing this is because I only want to fill text in the inputs that are of type

54
00:04:41,300 --> 00:04:41,960
text.

55
00:04:41,960 --> 00:04:46,130
So you can see that the text box right here is of type text.

56
00:04:46,370 --> 00:04:53,090
So I want to fill this with my payloads that will test for vulnerabilities and then maybe even fill

57
00:04:53,090 --> 00:04:56,840
them with exploits if I do discover an exploit.

58
00:04:57,200 --> 00:05:04,250
But if we look at the other input in here, this is just a button and it's type is submit.

59
00:05:04,310 --> 00:05:08,240
And therefore I don't want to change the value of this input.

60
00:05:09,110 --> 00:05:15,530
So I'm getting the input type at this line and I'm going to check if my input type.

61
00:05:16,720 --> 00:05:17,800
His text.

62
00:05:19,250 --> 00:05:24,230
Then for now, because we're testing, I'm just going to set the value.

63
00:05:24,740 --> 00:05:29,060
So we're going to say input value is equal to test.

64
00:05:30,560 --> 00:05:35,000
And actually in here, I'm going to set the default input value.

65
00:05:36,070 --> 00:05:43,870
What should be equal to the input does not get the value in the value attribute.

66
00:05:44,350 --> 00:05:51,760
So basically what we're saying is we're saying the input value is equal to the value that we have in

67
00:05:51,760 --> 00:05:53,770
the attribute field in here.

68
00:05:54,370 --> 00:06:01,780
But if the input is of type text similar to this one, this will be cached in this if statement.

69
00:06:01,780 --> 00:06:05,080
And if it is, we're going to set the value to test.

70
00:06:05,890 --> 00:06:12,490
And once we're done with all of this, we have the input name and we have the input value.

71
00:06:12,520 --> 00:06:18,820
All we have to do is add this to the dictionary that we will send as the post data.

72
00:06:19,480 --> 00:06:27,730
So before even entering this input, we're going to create our post data dictionary, which is going

73
00:06:27,730 --> 00:06:28,750
to be empty.

74
00:06:28,810 --> 00:06:31,330
Again, it's similar to what we did in here.

75
00:06:33,410 --> 00:06:37,800
And at the end of each time we pass an input.

76
00:06:37,820 --> 00:06:40,850
I'm going to say my post data.

77
00:06:42,390 --> 00:06:50,370
And the key this time is going to be the input name again, like we did in the guest login.

78
00:06:50,580 --> 00:07:00,240
So the input name is the key and here and the value for this is going to be the input value.

79
00:07:01,670 --> 00:07:09,260
Then once we exit this loop, this means that we have looped over all of our inputs each time, taken

80
00:07:09,260 --> 00:07:16,760
the input name and the input value, adding them to the post data dictionary set in the input name as

81
00:07:16,760 --> 00:07:20,720
the key and the input value as the value for this dictionary.

82
00:07:20,810 --> 00:07:24,710
Now we are ready to submit the form.

83
00:07:25,310 --> 00:07:31,760
So to submit the form, all we have to do is do requests dot post.

84
00:07:33,400 --> 00:07:37,240
And what we want to post is the post URL.

85
00:07:37,900 --> 00:07:39,940
So it's this variable right here.

86
00:07:40,820 --> 00:07:43,130
And we want to set the data.

87
00:07:44,000 --> 00:07:46,520
To my post data.

88
00:07:47,520 --> 00:07:49,710
Which is this dictionary right there.

89
00:07:50,930 --> 00:07:54,860
Now let me post this in here and see what we get.

90
00:07:55,850 --> 00:07:57,830
And as you can see, we get nothing.

91
00:07:57,830 --> 00:08:03,710
But basically the thing that will let us know that this has been submitted successfully is the fact

92
00:08:03,710 --> 00:08:09,890
that we see result for test because I typed test in my input box and here.

93
00:08:11,080 --> 00:08:21,010
So if I run this code and actually store the result of this in a variable and then print.

94
00:08:22,280 --> 00:08:23,390
The result.

95
00:08:24,300 --> 00:08:25,620
Dot content.

96
00:08:26,460 --> 00:08:29,910
Then I should get a HTML code of this.

97
00:08:29,910 --> 00:08:35,460
And the thing that I'm going to be looking for is results for test, which will mean that the values

98
00:08:35,460 --> 00:08:40,320
have been set properly as I want them and the form has been submitted properly.

99
00:08:41,380 --> 00:08:43,540
So I'm going to go down.

100
00:08:43,540 --> 00:08:46,270
We'll clear this and run the code.

101
00:08:48,640 --> 00:08:53,200
And as you can see in here, it's telling us this is the result for test.

102
00:08:53,350 --> 00:09:01,000
So basically it means that we successfully set the value of the input box to test and we successfully

103
00:09:01,000 --> 00:09:05,590
submitted the form and the web application accepted this form.

104
00:09:06,370 --> 00:09:15,520
So now once you learn about web application vulnerabilities such as SQL injections, access and so on,

105
00:09:15,550 --> 00:09:24,010
all you have to do is instead of submitting test, submit the exploit or the payload and then analyze

106
00:09:24,010 --> 00:09:28,510
and see if this got executed and achieved your result.

107
00:09:29,110 --> 00:09:35,830
Now, don't get too scared about this and don't get too intimidated by the way I'm implementing these.

108
00:09:35,980 --> 00:09:43,180
I'm actually just trying to show you a number of concepts so that in the next lectures we will put them

109
00:09:43,180 --> 00:09:49,030
all together to build a program that will automatically discover exploits for us.

110
00:09:50,490 --> 00:09:57,090
Like I said, it's very, very important to keep in mind that you can use beautiful soup, just like

111
00:09:57,090 --> 00:10:02,940
I did before, to extract any HTML element from any HTML page.

112
00:10:02,970 --> 00:10:12,270
Not only that, you've also seen how powerful it is to extract specific attributes or specific options

113
00:10:12,270 --> 00:10:17,670
that you can see within the HTML tag or within the HTML element.

114
00:10:18,300 --> 00:10:26,730
So all of this can be adapted and used in any scenario where you need to read data from HTML pages.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,860 --> 00:00:07,820
Right now, I think you have enough knowledge to start building your own vulnerability scanner.

2
00:00:08,390 --> 00:00:10,040
Now I know what you're thinking.

3
00:00:10,070 --> 00:00:14,450
I didn't teach you how to discover any type of vulnerabilities yet.

4
00:00:14,810 --> 00:00:15,380
Sure.

5
00:00:15,380 --> 00:00:19,070
I didn't even explain what a vulnerability is.

6
00:00:19,970 --> 00:00:27,290
Well, basically, a vulnerability is a weakness that can be used to hack the target website the users,

7
00:00:27,290 --> 00:00:30,560
or get more information about the website or the users.

8
00:00:31,280 --> 00:00:36,520
Now, there is a number of vulnerabilities and there is a number of ways to discover and exploit them.

9
00:00:36,530 --> 00:00:40,190
But the general idea is the same.

10
00:00:40,190 --> 00:00:47,210
And that's why I want to show you how to build a generic vulnerability scanner that can be used to discover

11
00:00:47,210 --> 00:00:49,310
a large number of vulnerabilities.

12
00:00:49,340 --> 00:00:54,470
So, first of all, I'm going to show you how to build this scanner and then I'll show you how easy

13
00:00:54,470 --> 00:00:59,990
it is to extend this generic scanner to discover different types of vulnerabilities.

14
00:01:00,740 --> 00:01:06,290
See the general steps to discover any vulnerability, regardless of what it is, is, first of all,

15
00:01:06,290 --> 00:01:07,910
you need to make a sitemap.

16
00:01:07,910 --> 00:01:12,500
So you need to go into every possible page, and we've built something that can do that.

17
00:01:12,770 --> 00:01:17,030
We need to analyze this page and extract the URLs and the forms.

18
00:01:17,450 --> 00:01:20,810
And then we have two very important steps.

19
00:01:21,260 --> 00:01:28,940
The first one is to send payloads to discover the vulnerability, and the next important step is to

20
00:01:28,940 --> 00:01:35,930
analyze the response that we got after sending the payload and see if this website is vulnerable.

21
00:01:36,890 --> 00:01:43,310
So as I said, the general steps are always the same regardless of what vulnerability we're trying to

22
00:01:43,310 --> 00:01:44,120
discover.

23
00:01:44,120 --> 00:01:51,230
And the only thing that we change is the things in red, so the payload that we send and the response

24
00:01:51,230 --> 00:01:52,460
that we look for.

25
00:01:53,240 --> 00:01:58,820
So right now and in the next few lectures, I want to build a program that can, first of all, make

26
00:01:58,820 --> 00:02:04,600
a map of the target website, extract the links and the forms, send a test payload.

27
00:02:04,610 --> 00:02:10,130
So we're literally just going to send the string test and then we're just going to analyze the result

28
00:02:10,130 --> 00:02:11,810
that we get as is.

29
00:02:11,930 --> 00:02:19,100
And then I'll show you how easy it is to just write a simple method to discover a specific vulnerability.

30
00:02:19,100 --> 00:02:23,870
And then you'll be able to write methods for all the different types of vulnerabilities.

31
00:02:23,870 --> 00:02:29,990
So later on, all you'll have to do is literally just extend the base code that we're going to write.

32
00:02:30,860 --> 00:02:34,370
So let's go ahead and start cracking at this.

33
00:02:34,410 --> 00:02:41,080
And right now, I've already created a project in my Passion Projects directory.

34
00:02:41,090 --> 00:02:43,250
It's called Vulnerability Scanner.

35
00:02:43,270 --> 00:02:46,180
And as you can see, there is two main files.

36
00:02:46,190 --> 00:02:49,760
I have the scanner dot API and I have the vulnerability.

37
00:02:49,760 --> 00:02:52,910
Is Canada pi the scanner.

38
00:02:53,150 --> 00:03:01,520
PI is in here and as you can see, it is a class in which I'm going to implement most of the code that

39
00:03:01,520 --> 00:03:03,920
will do the hard work for us.

40
00:03:04,460 --> 00:03:11,060
Then we have the vulnerability scanner, which is going to be a very, very simple program that literally

41
00:03:11,060 --> 00:03:15,470
calls the class and executes the code that's implemented in it.

42
00:03:16,580 --> 00:03:20,090
So as you can see, I've already created the class.

43
00:03:20,090 --> 00:03:26,420
And the first thing that I want to do is create my init method, which is the method that gets executed

44
00:03:26,420 --> 00:03:30,200
as soon as an instance of this class is created.

45
00:03:30,380 --> 00:03:35,860
I cover this in more details before when we when we spoke about object oriented programming.

46
00:03:35,870 --> 00:03:39,470
So if you don't remember this, please go back and revise that lecture.

47
00:03:40,040 --> 00:03:42,560
So basically, we're just going to do def.

48
00:03:43,300 --> 00:03:47,110
Underscore, underscore in it, underscore, underscore.

49
00:03:48,040 --> 00:03:54,280
And as we know, this is going to be a scanner that will scan a certain website for vulnerabilities.

50
00:03:54,280 --> 00:04:01,750
So I'm going to make this take a URL as an input, and I'm going to store this in a variable inside

51
00:04:01,750 --> 00:04:02,530
my class.

52
00:04:02,530 --> 00:04:06,070
So we're going to say self dot.

53
00:04:06,850 --> 00:04:10,780
Target your URL is equal to URL.

54
00:04:11,410 --> 00:04:17,680
So now throughout all of the methods that I'm going to build in my class, I'll be able to access the

55
00:04:17,680 --> 00:04:22,210
URL by saying self dot target URL.

56
00:04:23,730 --> 00:04:29,760
Now going back to the steps that we need to do, the first thing that we need to do, even as humans,

57
00:04:29,760 --> 00:04:32,220
is to go into every possible page.

58
00:04:32,220 --> 00:04:38,340
So basically build a site map of the target website or crawl the target website.

59
00:04:38,910 --> 00:04:42,550
And we already have a program that can do that for us.

60
00:04:42,570 --> 00:04:45,360
It's the crawler program that we built before.

61
00:04:46,320 --> 00:04:51,540
So what I'm actually going to do, I'm actually going to copy all of this code as is.

62
00:04:52,140 --> 00:04:53,280
And it should work.

63
00:04:53,280 --> 00:04:59,010
I'll just have to modify it slightly because I'm going to use it inside a class right now.

64
00:05:00,150 --> 00:05:02,160
So I'm going to maximize this.

65
00:05:02,160 --> 00:05:09,420
And the first thing that we need to do in class methods is they have to take self as the first argument.

66
00:05:09,930 --> 00:05:15,570
And again, I spoke about this before, so if you don't remember any of this, please go back to the

67
00:05:15,570 --> 00:05:19,290
lecture where I speak about object oriented programming.

68
00:05:20,160 --> 00:05:26,490
Now looking at the extract links from looks like everything is fine here except the fact that we need

69
00:05:26,490 --> 00:05:29,910
to import the real library because it's used in here.

70
00:05:30,180 --> 00:05:33,900
So I'm just going to do in here import re.

71
00:05:35,080 --> 00:05:36,940
So this should be okay.

72
00:05:36,940 --> 00:05:38,380
So I'm going to minimize it.

73
00:05:39,100 --> 00:05:42,760
And the next method that we need to fix now is the crawl.

74
00:05:42,760 --> 00:05:48,730
And you can see the first thing that's underlined in red is the extract links from now.

75
00:05:48,730 --> 00:05:54,880
We were using this in a function so we could call it like this, but right now we're using it in a class

76
00:05:54,880 --> 00:05:59,020
and extract links from is a method in the class.

77
00:05:59,260 --> 00:06:04,960
So when we want to call it, we have to say self dot the method name.

78
00:06:06,460 --> 00:06:10,540
The next thing that's highlighted for us is the URL pass.

79
00:06:10,570 --> 00:06:15,070
Again, that's a library that we haven't imported here, so I'm just going to import it.

80
00:06:17,700 --> 00:06:25,080
The next thing that's underlined in red is target URL and here that references the main target URL that

81
00:06:25,080 --> 00:06:31,680
we start looking from again that was used inside our function so we could just access it by sending

82
00:06:31,710 --> 00:06:32,940
target URL.

83
00:06:33,390 --> 00:06:39,330
Right now in our class, our target URL is named as self the target URL.

84
00:06:39,360 --> 00:06:42,090
So I just need to add self to this.

85
00:06:43,430 --> 00:06:52,040
And in here this is referencing my target links list, which is the list that contains all of the links

86
00:06:52,190 --> 00:06:54,800
extracted from the target URL.

87
00:06:55,550 --> 00:07:01,160
Now in our program, we had that defined in here the target links.

88
00:07:02,150 --> 00:07:07,940
So I'll actually need to create one in here because I haven't created one and I'm going to create it

89
00:07:07,940 --> 00:07:10,130
in my initialize method.

90
00:07:10,790 --> 00:07:19,400
So I'm going to say self dot target links is equal to an empty list.

91
00:07:20,210 --> 00:07:27,230
So all we have to do is just add self in here now and that'll reference this variable that we just created.

92
00:07:27,320 --> 00:07:28,910
So that's perfect now.

93
00:07:29,690 --> 00:07:34,820
And we'll also have to add self in here for the method that we created.

94
00:07:35,990 --> 00:07:45,020
And finally, when the crop calls itself, we need to add self before that to make it call itself again

95
00:07:45,020 --> 00:07:47,150
because that was used as a function.

96
00:07:47,150 --> 00:07:52,790
So we didn't need to add self, but because it is inside the method, we had to add the self before

97
00:07:52,790 --> 00:07:53,180
it.

98
00:07:54,050 --> 00:07:55,580
So that's perfect for now.

99
00:07:55,580 --> 00:07:57,890
We didn't really program anything new.

100
00:07:58,010 --> 00:08:04,980
All we did is we copy pasted our crawler code inside the new scanner class that we created.

101
00:08:05,000 --> 00:08:09,980
The only new thing is the instance variables and the structure of the object.

102
00:08:10,790 --> 00:08:17,390
So now in my vulnerability scanner, I've already imported my scanner, which is this one right here.

103
00:08:18,320 --> 00:08:28,130
And the next thing that I'm going to do is I'm going to say my vulnerability scanner is equal to scanner.

104
00:08:28,160 --> 00:08:30,770
DOT scanner.

105
00:08:33,650 --> 00:08:40,040
And I should have set this as capital S because as we know, class names should start with a capital

106
00:08:40,040 --> 00:08:40,610
letter.

107
00:08:42,480 --> 00:08:47,470
And this will take an input of the URL of my target.

108
00:08:47,490 --> 00:08:49,620
So let's actually create a variable.

109
00:08:49,620 --> 00:08:52,110
We'll call it target Earl.

110
00:08:52,350 --> 00:08:55,740
And this is going to be utility in here.

111
00:08:57,700 --> 00:09:02,050
And we'll pass this as an input to my scanner.

112
00:09:02,730 --> 00:09:06,960
This will be saved in the instance variable of target URL.

113
00:09:06,960 --> 00:09:07,680
And here.

114
00:09:08,310 --> 00:09:13,950
And the next thing that I want to do, I'm going to call the crawl method to make a list of all the

115
00:09:13,950 --> 00:09:16,710
possible links in my target website.

116
00:09:17,070 --> 00:09:23,520
As we know, by the end of this method, everything is going to be stored in my target links because

117
00:09:23,520 --> 00:09:29,880
we're appending it in here at this line, but it will also print it on screen for us, which is pretty

118
00:09:29,880 --> 00:09:30,230
nice.

119
00:09:30,240 --> 00:09:32,940
It lets us know that everything is working properly.

120
00:09:33,780 --> 00:09:38,610
So right now the VON Scanner is an instance of this scanner class.

121
00:09:39,000 --> 00:09:47,220
So I can say want scanner dot crawl to call the crawl method and I'm going to pass it again the target

122
00:09:47,340 --> 00:09:48,090
URL.

123
00:09:48,860 --> 00:09:54,710
Because as we know, this method takes a URL in which it should start crawling from.

124
00:09:55,400 --> 00:09:57,270
Now I know this is not very pretty.

125
00:09:57,290 --> 00:10:01,130
We're passing the target URL twice and I'll address this soon.

126
00:10:01,130 --> 00:10:04,850
But let's test this code like this and see if it works.

127
00:10:05,650 --> 00:10:08,140
So I'm going to go to my terminal.

128
00:10:08,170 --> 00:10:15,460
I'm already in my working directory and I'm just going to do Python Vulnerability Scanner Spy.

129
00:10:17,550 --> 00:10:22,080
Now it's complaining about my constructor not taking any inputs.

130
00:10:25,960 --> 00:10:29,380
And the main reason is I misspelled in it in here.

131
00:10:29,380 --> 00:10:31,370
I don't know how I achieved that.

132
00:10:31,390 --> 00:10:42,220
It's actually I n i t and now if we go down and run it again, we have a problem in line 17 in the scanner.

133
00:10:42,610 --> 00:10:44,650
So let's have a look again.

134
00:10:45,220 --> 00:10:51,850
And yeah, the problem is in here, the extract links from should only take a URL as an input.

135
00:10:51,880 --> 00:10:53,200
It shouldn't take itself.

136
00:10:53,200 --> 00:10:55,150
I don't know how I put that in there.

137
00:10:55,150 --> 00:10:57,220
It must have been autocomplete.

138
00:10:58,070 --> 00:11:00,680
So let's try it again now.

139
00:11:01,070 --> 00:11:01,940
And perfect.

140
00:11:01,940 --> 00:11:05,510
As you can see, the spider is working as expected.

141
00:11:05,510 --> 00:11:09,920
It's making a list of all the possible links on the Target website.

142
00:11:10,830 --> 00:11:18,450
And now all of these links are going to be stored inside my self dot target links, which I'll be able

143
00:11:18,450 --> 00:11:23,940
to access throughout all of the other methods that I'm going to implement in the next lectures.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:01,290 --> 00:00:01,890
Okay.

2
00:00:01,950 --> 00:00:09,060
Now that we're sure our program works fine and we're able to call the control method from our scanner

3
00:00:09,060 --> 00:00:16,110
function and it's able to get us all the links and then store them in my target links variable.

4
00:00:16,500 --> 00:00:19,290
I want to address one minor issue.

5
00:00:20,040 --> 00:00:26,850
As you can see, when I first create my object, the vulnerability scanner object, I am giving it the

6
00:00:26,850 --> 00:00:29,210
target URL, which is great.

7
00:00:29,250 --> 00:00:37,200
The target URL is going to be stored in the self, the target URL in the class and I can access this

8
00:00:37,200 --> 00:00:39,660
anywhere I want in my class.

9
00:00:40,320 --> 00:00:46,770
But if we look at my code again, when I call the crawl, I give it the target URL again.

10
00:00:46,770 --> 00:00:53,280
And this doesn't really make sense because I want to only give the target URL once when I create the

11
00:00:53,280 --> 00:00:54,060
object.

12
00:00:54,180 --> 00:01:00,840
And then when I call the crawl, the crawl should automatically know that it should start from the target

13
00:01:00,840 --> 00:01:08,760
URL that has been given to the class because the value of this is already stored in self dot target

14
00:01:08,760 --> 00:01:09,570
URL.

15
00:01:10,020 --> 00:01:12,750
Now you might think that doing this is very easy.

16
00:01:12,750 --> 00:01:20,040
All I have to do is remove the URL from here and every time I use URL in the code I'm going to use self

17
00:01:20,040 --> 00:01:21,450
to target URL.

18
00:01:22,350 --> 00:01:30,420
Now this would be the case if this function wasn't a recursive function because this function is a recursive

19
00:01:30,420 --> 00:01:31,200
function.

20
00:01:31,350 --> 00:01:33,750
It is calling itself in here.

21
00:01:34,320 --> 00:01:39,180
And the way that it calls itself, it calls itself with the current link.

22
00:01:39,180 --> 00:01:45,120
So every time it calls itself, it's going to call itself on a completely new link.

23
00:01:45,810 --> 00:01:54,960
So if I use self the target URL in here, then I will not be able to use this function to discover all

24
00:01:54,960 --> 00:01:57,000
the links in all the web pages.

25
00:01:57,000 --> 00:02:03,150
It will only discover the links in one web page, which is the one in the self target URL.

26
00:02:04,080 --> 00:02:10,770
So what I'm going to do today is actually introduce you to something that is new, simple and useful,

27
00:02:10,770 --> 00:02:15,150
which is using a default value for an argument.

28
00:02:15,630 --> 00:02:22,830
So I'm going to keep my crawl method to take a URL argument, but I'm going to say the default value

29
00:02:22,830 --> 00:02:25,260
for the URL is none.

30
00:02:26,370 --> 00:02:35,340
Which basically means I can call this method without specifying a URL and the method will set the value

31
00:02:35,340 --> 00:02:37,140
of the URL to none.

32
00:02:38,210 --> 00:02:43,960
So this will allow me to call the method like so without specifying a URL.

33
00:02:43,970 --> 00:02:48,620
And in this case the value of the URL is going to be set to none.

34
00:02:49,610 --> 00:02:54,170
And I can call it with specifying a URL like in here.

35
00:02:55,040 --> 00:03:01,850
And in this case the value of the URL is going to be the value passed to the method.

36
00:03:02,630 --> 00:03:04,820
Now we still have one problem.

37
00:03:04,820 --> 00:03:11,960
If we call the crawl like in here without specifying a URL, then the value for the URL is going to

38
00:03:11,960 --> 00:03:16,590
be set to the default value, which is none because I'm specifying it here as none.

39
00:03:16,610 --> 00:03:20,750
You can specify it as anything you want, but I'm setting it as non.

40
00:03:21,410 --> 00:03:27,290
Then this is going to be used in my methods in here and throughout the code in here as well.

41
00:03:28,090 --> 00:03:31,570
And because this is none, it will actually crash my code.

42
00:03:32,380 --> 00:03:35,050
So I'm going to add a check in here.

43
00:03:35,050 --> 00:03:44,710
I'm going to say if my URL is equal to none, then this means this method has been called from outside

44
00:03:44,710 --> 00:03:48,250
of this class, not from the recursive call in here.

45
00:03:48,250 --> 00:03:53,470
And that means I should start from the target URL.

46
00:03:53,740 --> 00:04:00,880
So I'm going to say my URL is equal to self dot target URL.

47
00:04:01,730 --> 00:04:08,990
So in this case, if the method gets called without specifying a URL, the URL is going to be set to

48
00:04:08,990 --> 00:04:15,590
self the target URL, which is the URL that the user sets the target to when they create the scanner.

49
00:04:16,310 --> 00:04:22,070
Otherwise, if the URL is passed to the method, then the URL is going to be directly used in the method

50
00:04:22,070 --> 00:04:24,350
as the value that is passed to it.

51
00:04:25,100 --> 00:04:31,760
This will make our code nicer because right now we can create the object like so passing it the target

52
00:04:31,760 --> 00:04:37,970
URL and then I can just say I want my vulnerability scanner to start crawling.

53
00:04:38,810 --> 00:04:41,810
Now let's go and test this and make sure we didn't break anything.

54
00:04:44,130 --> 00:04:44,760
And perfect.

55
00:04:44,760 --> 00:04:51,330
As you can see, it's working as expected, given us a similar result to what we were getting before.

56
00:04:51,930 --> 00:04:54,750
So it's just a new and nice tip to know.

57
00:04:55,050 --> 00:05:00,660
It's very handy and you can use it anywhere, not only inside methods, you can use it with functions,

58
00:05:00,660 --> 00:05:03,720
and you can set a default value to anything you want.

59
00:05:03,750 --> 00:05:08,160
I'm just setting it to none because it makes sense to me in this example.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:01,330 --> 00:00:08,740
Now I'd also like to add a new feature to our crawler so we know that it works perfectly with pretty

2
00:00:08,740 --> 00:00:16,240
much all websites, but it's going to have a problem with websites that require you to log in, or maybe

3
00:00:16,240 --> 00:00:19,810
you have a normal website such as security.

4
00:00:19,810 --> 00:00:25,420
But also if you log in, you will have more functionality such as the user page.

5
00:00:25,420 --> 00:00:31,990
So that page might contain links and vulnerabilities, but our crawler is going to miss that because

6
00:00:31,990 --> 00:00:34,360
it won't be able to log in.

7
00:00:34,960 --> 00:00:36,970
Now, you might think this is simple.

8
00:00:37,000 --> 00:00:43,630
All you have to do is just use the code that we used to log in to this website, which is the code that

9
00:00:43,630 --> 00:00:45,880
I have right here in the Post program.

10
00:00:45,880 --> 00:00:52,690
So we create a dictionary of the inputs, we put the username and the password and the input button,

11
00:00:53,110 --> 00:00:57,580
and then we send them by a post request with the parameters.

12
00:00:58,150 --> 00:01:03,670
But this will not work because when you send this request, you will log in.

13
00:01:03,670 --> 00:01:05,260
That's, that's fine.

14
00:01:05,470 --> 00:01:12,040
But once the request is over and you get the response, you will automatically be logged out.

15
00:01:12,040 --> 00:01:17,680
So you can think of it as if you close the browser and you delete all the history and cookies and all

16
00:01:17,680 --> 00:01:18,370
of that.

17
00:01:19,490 --> 00:01:20,570
Let me show you.

18
00:01:20,720 --> 00:01:29,120
So I'm just going to copy all of this and let's put it in our program and we're going to log in before

19
00:01:29,120 --> 00:01:34,040
we start the vulnerability scanner and then we'll start scanning after that.

20
00:01:34,580 --> 00:01:41,930
So I'm going to remove this variable here for the target URL and I'm just going to set the target to

21
00:01:41,930 --> 00:01:43,410
div w a.

22
00:01:44,090 --> 00:01:47,000
I'll also need to import requests in here.

23
00:01:49,740 --> 00:01:52,610
And this code should work fine now.

24
00:01:52,620 --> 00:01:54,330
But let's go and test it.

25
00:01:55,800 --> 00:02:02,850
And as you can see, it's able to get me only one link, which is a link to a stylesheet in the login

26
00:02:02,850 --> 00:02:03,510
page.

27
00:02:04,050 --> 00:02:06,380
So basically the code is working fine.

28
00:02:06,390 --> 00:02:10,410
It's able to get all the links in here, but there is only one link.

29
00:02:10,950 --> 00:02:18,180
And even though we gave it code to log in so we know that this code right here will allow me to log

30
00:02:18,180 --> 00:02:18,420
in.

31
00:02:18,420 --> 00:02:22,980
I'm given the right username and the right password and we tested this before.

32
00:02:23,040 --> 00:02:30,140
We're still not able to log in because this login process is fine and it will be successful.

33
00:02:30,150 --> 00:02:37,500
But by the end of it you can imagine that as if you're going to log in here as a human normally and

34
00:02:37,500 --> 00:02:40,320
then you will close the browser and delete the history.

35
00:02:40,710 --> 00:02:47,340
So every time you make a new request after that to extract all the links, you will be logged out and

36
00:02:47,340 --> 00:02:49,380
you won't be able to get the links.

37
00:02:50,360 --> 00:02:53,210
Now the solution to this is actually very easy.

38
00:02:53,660 --> 00:02:55,700
So let me maximize this again.

39
00:02:55,910 --> 00:03:02,900
And what I'm going to do in my scanner, as soon as we create this kind of object, I'm going to create

40
00:03:02,900 --> 00:03:03,950
a session.

41
00:03:04,550 --> 00:03:07,760
So I'm going to say self that session.

42
00:03:09,550 --> 00:03:14,920
Is equal to requests that session.

43
00:03:15,670 --> 00:03:22,780
So what we're doing is we're creating a session object which will represent our current session.

44
00:03:23,770 --> 00:03:31,240
Then throughout my program, every time I want to send a request, whether it's a post or a get request,

45
00:03:31,250 --> 00:03:38,230
instead of doing requests dot method name, we're going to say self dot session followed by the method

46
00:03:38,230 --> 00:03:38,770
name.

47
00:03:39,760 --> 00:03:46,090
So right now in my program, I only have one call to requests and I'm using a get method.

48
00:03:46,240 --> 00:03:51,370
But instead of calling the requests library like this, I'm going to do self.

49
00:03:52,510 --> 00:03:53,530
The session.

50
00:03:55,110 --> 00:03:59,820
And then we can use the dot gate and the dot post as we've seen them before.

51
00:04:00,780 --> 00:04:08,850
The advantage of this is as soon as we create the object in here at this line, we will create a session.

52
00:04:08,850 --> 00:04:13,200
So you can think of this as if we are opening a web browser.

53
00:04:13,950 --> 00:04:19,350
So then everything I do, it will be done throughout the session.

54
00:04:19,620 --> 00:04:26,640
So right here, whenever I'm logging in, I'm going to copy this line and paste it here.

55
00:04:26,640 --> 00:04:32,790
And again, instead of calling requests dot post, what I'm going to say is I'm going to say I want

56
00:04:32,790 --> 00:04:43,320
to call my vulnerability scanner dot session and then to this session I want to post the following data

57
00:04:43,320 --> 00:04:45,270
to the following URL.

58
00:04:45,990 --> 00:04:53,400
So this data will be used to log in to the login page, which is with a username of admin and a password

59
00:04:53,400 --> 00:04:54,360
of password.

60
00:04:54,570 --> 00:05:01,830
And then once I log in, I will not lose that session because it will be stored in the session variable

61
00:05:01,830 --> 00:05:04,500
in self session in here.

62
00:05:05,600 --> 00:05:14,450
Then I can send requests as post or get to my target by doing self dot session followed by the method

63
00:05:14,450 --> 00:05:15,920
that I want to use.

64
00:05:16,010 --> 00:05:23,270
This will allow me to send these requests from that session so if I log in I will always be logged in

65
00:05:23,270 --> 00:05:25,910
unless I actually go and log out.

66
00:05:26,970 --> 00:05:33,180
Now I should actually put the login URL in here instead of the target url.

67
00:05:33,630 --> 00:05:39,120
So it's going to be db w followed by log in dot php.

68
00:05:40,260 --> 00:05:45,360
And I also don't need to stir the result of this anywhere because I don't really want to use it.

69
00:05:45,360 --> 00:05:51,060
I just want to log in and I'm not going to need the requests library in here anymore.

70
00:05:51,870 --> 00:05:54,630
So let's go ahead and test this code.

71
00:05:56,770 --> 00:05:57,720
And perfect.

72
00:05:57,730 --> 00:06:03,970
It is logging in and it's actually getting me more links than I was getting before, which means that

73
00:06:03,970 --> 00:06:11,320
it's able to log in and it's able to persist its session and send requests as a logged in user.

74
00:06:12,380 --> 00:06:20,150
Now speaking of login out, you can see that one of the links that we detected is a log out dot PHP.

75
00:06:20,510 --> 00:06:26,930
And basically when you submit a request to this file, it will log you out of the website.

76
00:06:27,650 --> 00:06:34,400
So this means you won't be able to send any requests to the website again without logging in.

77
00:06:35,120 --> 00:06:40,670
Now, this will be a problem because because this link is being added to my target lists.

78
00:06:40,700 --> 00:06:48,110
This means that whenever I run any scan to this link, it will actually log me out of the session and

79
00:06:48,110 --> 00:06:51,590
I won't be able to do anything anymore on the website.

80
00:06:52,310 --> 00:07:00,710
So what I'm actually going to do is I'm going to create a variable here after my target URL and we're

81
00:07:00,710 --> 00:07:04,880
going to call this links to ignore.

82
00:07:05,900 --> 00:07:13,400
And basically, I want this to be a list in which each element is a link that I want to tell my program

83
00:07:13,400 --> 00:07:16,610
to ignore and not send the requests to.

84
00:07:17,390 --> 00:07:19,520
So I'm going to copy this.

85
00:07:21,090 --> 00:07:26,460
And put it as the first element in this list, and it's the only element in our case.

86
00:07:27,120 --> 00:07:31,440
Then I want to pass this list to my vulnerability scanner.

87
00:07:31,770 --> 00:07:34,320
So we're going to pass it in here.

88
00:07:34,500 --> 00:07:35,700
Links to ignore.

89
00:07:36,780 --> 00:07:42,330
And then I'm going to modify my constructor method to take this as an input.

90
00:07:42,990 --> 00:07:45,780
So we'll call it ignore links.

91
00:07:46,140 --> 00:07:57,870
And in here I'm going to say my self dot links to ignore is equal to the ignore links so that in my

92
00:07:57,870 --> 00:08:03,270
program now I can access this list by saying self dot links to ignore.

93
00:08:04,140 --> 00:08:10,590
Then the main place that I actually want to ignore them is where I do the crawling, which is this call.

94
00:08:10,590 --> 00:08:11,340
And here.

95
00:08:12,170 --> 00:08:17,600
So this call checks if this link is not an external link, if it is unique.

96
00:08:17,600 --> 00:08:20,990
And I also want to check if the link.

97
00:08:22,390 --> 00:08:23,680
Is not.

98
00:08:23,850 --> 00:08:28,420
MN myself dot links to ignore.

99
00:08:29,350 --> 00:08:35,440
Therefore, if we reach this line and we have a unique link that is a link on my target website.

100
00:08:35,440 --> 00:08:40,210
I will also check if this link is in my list to ignore.

101
00:08:40,240 --> 00:08:46,600
If it is, I want crawl this link and I won't even make a request to it and I won't even store it in

102
00:08:46,600 --> 00:08:47,710
my target links.

103
00:08:47,710 --> 00:08:49,960
Because this is not a target link.

104
00:08:49,990 --> 00:08:54,010
It is actually a link in my links to ignore list.

105
00:08:55,420 --> 00:08:59,170
So if we just run the program again, it should work fine.

106
00:08:59,170 --> 00:09:05,110
But this will actually fix a problem that we would face in the future and you would actually face even

107
00:09:05,110 --> 00:09:07,060
in crawling and other websites.

108
00:09:07,060 --> 00:09:13,720
But it's not an issue for this particular website because we're able to get all the links before crawling

109
00:09:13,720 --> 00:09:14,980
the log out link.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:01,500 --> 00:00:07,450
So by now we finished the first step and most of the second step.

2
00:00:07,470 --> 00:00:15,650
So we have a program that we can use to make a list of all the possible targets on our target website.

3
00:00:15,660 --> 00:00:17,970
We will extract all the links.

4
00:00:18,030 --> 00:00:22,560
And from these links we can obviously send data if there is an equal in the link.

5
00:00:23,250 --> 00:00:29,190
The second part of the second point is to be able to extract the forms as well.

6
00:00:30,090 --> 00:00:36,920
Now we already built a program that can extract forms and we spoke about how this works in details.

7
00:00:36,930 --> 00:00:41,700
So what I'm going to do, I'm actually going to start copying code from here into my class.

8
00:00:41,700 --> 00:00:45,870
And as usual, I'll have to modify a few things to get it to work.

9
00:00:46,410 --> 00:00:53,640
So first I'll need to import beautiful soup because that's what's used in order to extract the forms.

10
00:00:54,830 --> 00:01:00,260
And then I'd like to have a method in here called Extract.

11
00:01:01,490 --> 00:01:02,450
Forums.

12
00:01:03,480 --> 00:01:12,540
And this method would take a URL and when it finishes execution, I wanted to return all the forms in

13
00:01:12,540 --> 00:01:13,980
the given URL.

14
00:01:14,700 --> 00:01:19,650
Now we already have something that will do that which is implemented in here.

15
00:01:19,800 --> 00:01:22,350
So I'm just going to copy these three lines.

16
00:01:23,040 --> 00:01:24,330
I'm going to paste them here.

17
00:01:24,330 --> 00:01:27,680
And I spoke about how to do this in details in their lecture.

18
00:01:27,690 --> 00:01:31,830
So I'm not really going to go into details about how these three lines work.

19
00:01:32,250 --> 00:01:36,390
If you don't remember how they work, please go back and revise the lecture.

20
00:01:36,720 --> 00:01:41,510
Basically, right here, we make a request to the target URL.

21
00:01:41,520 --> 00:01:49,610
So in my object I'll need to do a self dot session to make that request through my session.

22
00:01:49,620 --> 00:01:56,400
And this is going to be a get request and this request should be made to the variable that we're getting

23
00:01:56,400 --> 00:02:00,810
here as an input, which is called URL, not target URL.

24
00:02:01,740 --> 00:02:08,520
Then we're using beautiful soup to parse the response that we get from the above request.

25
00:02:08,880 --> 00:02:16,350
We're starting the result in a variable called parsed HTML, and then we are using a find all method

26
00:02:16,350 --> 00:02:20,130
to find all the elements that has a form tag.

27
00:02:21,050 --> 00:02:28,190
Now, instead of saving this in a variable, I'm going to return all the form tags that we discover.

28
00:02:28,970 --> 00:02:30,140
And thus this method.

29
00:02:30,140 --> 00:02:30,650
Done.

30
00:02:31,910 --> 00:02:36,950
The next thing that I want to do is to be able to submit these forms.

31
00:02:36,950 --> 00:02:40,730
So I'd like to have a method called submit form.

32
00:02:43,170 --> 00:02:49,830
And I'd like this method to take the form that we want to submit, the value that we want to set the

33
00:02:49,830 --> 00:02:59,040
inputs to in this form, and the URL of the page that this form was extracted from.

34
00:02:59,220 --> 00:03:03,660
This is important to know where we're going to submit this form to.

35
00:03:04,710 --> 00:03:09,780
Then I actually have code in here that we use to submit stuff to a form.

36
00:03:10,530 --> 00:03:13,200
So again, I'm going to copy all of this.

37
00:03:14,560 --> 00:03:17,110
And I'm going to paste it in here.

38
00:03:18,420 --> 00:03:23,460
And going through this code quickly, you can see it's going to extract the action.

39
00:03:23,760 --> 00:03:28,840
It's going to join the target URL with the action.

40
00:03:28,860 --> 00:03:34,250
Now, in this example, the target URL was the URL, which we got the form from.

41
00:03:34,260 --> 00:03:41,130
But in this example, the URL where we get the form from is going to be passed to this method in the

42
00:03:41,130 --> 00:03:42,330
URL variable.

43
00:03:42,330 --> 00:03:44,640
So this is going to be the URL in here.

44
00:03:45,700 --> 00:03:52,900
Then we're extracting the method so forms can be sent using, get or post and we're extracting it in

45
00:03:52,900 --> 00:03:53,500
here.

46
00:03:54,070 --> 00:03:57,590
Then we're extracting all the inputs in this form.

47
00:03:57,610 --> 00:04:00,490
We're iterating over them one at a time.

48
00:04:01,060 --> 00:04:05,440
We're getting the name, the type, and the value of the form.

49
00:04:05,680 --> 00:04:13,360
And then if the form is a text, if it's a text box, we're setting its value to test.

50
00:04:13,660 --> 00:04:19,390
So I actually like to set its value to the value that we pass to this method.

51
00:04:19,900 --> 00:04:23,380
So I'm going to remove test and set that to value.

52
00:04:24,830 --> 00:04:32,690
And finally, we're adding this input to our dictionary, which we then send as a post request.

53
00:04:33,970 --> 00:04:34,330
Now.

54
00:04:34,330 --> 00:04:37,700
I actually don't want to stir the results in a result variable.

55
00:04:37,720 --> 00:04:40,090
I'm just going to return the result.

56
00:04:42,920 --> 00:04:50,810
Now, one thing that I want to modify in this code is we are sending the result at the end as opposed

57
00:04:50,810 --> 00:04:51,640
to request.

58
00:04:51,650 --> 00:04:57,740
And like I said, most forms use post as their method, but not all.

59
00:04:57,950 --> 00:05:00,440
So let me show you an example in here.

60
00:05:01,280 --> 00:05:10,340
If you go to DV W A and go to X as reflected, we have a form here that is vulnerable to a vulnerability

61
00:05:10,340 --> 00:05:11,570
called XSS.

62
00:05:11,870 --> 00:05:16,520
And if we just right click this form and inspect the element.

63
00:05:17,760 --> 00:05:21,210
You can see that the method in this form is get.

64
00:05:22,020 --> 00:05:29,400
So our code is going to extract this form, set its value to the value that we pass it, but then it

65
00:05:29,400 --> 00:05:36,060
will submit this value to this form using post, which is incorrect and it will actually not work.

66
00:05:36,840 --> 00:05:45,060
So what I want to do in here, I am already extracting the method from the form tag and it's stored

67
00:05:45,060 --> 00:05:46,710
in a variable called method.

68
00:05:47,520 --> 00:05:51,630
So I'm going to check if method is equal to post.

69
00:05:51,960 --> 00:05:55,800
If it is, then we're going to send this as a post request.

70
00:05:56,800 --> 00:06:04,900
And this should be sent and self the session so that it's sent as part of my session, not as a new

71
00:06:04,900 --> 00:06:05,770
request.

72
00:06:06,430 --> 00:06:11,230
And if the method is not posed, this will not be executed.

73
00:06:11,230 --> 00:06:21,490
And what we're going to return is self dot session dot get and we're going to give it the post URL.

74
00:06:22,390 --> 00:06:27,280
And this time, instead of saying, let me just increase the size of this.

75
00:06:27,490 --> 00:06:33,760
And instead of saying data is equal to the post data, we're going to say params.

76
00:06:35,910 --> 00:06:38,700
Are equal to my post data.

77
00:06:39,860 --> 00:06:43,530
So you can see the arguments are actually very similar.

78
00:06:43,550 --> 00:06:49,400
The only difference is we're checking if the method is post, then we're going to return the result

79
00:06:49,400 --> 00:06:53,680
of posting the post URL with the posted data.

80
00:06:53,690 --> 00:06:56,780
But we're given that to the data argument.

81
00:06:57,620 --> 00:07:04,610
Otherwise we're going to return the result of sending a get request with the post URL and we're sending

82
00:07:04,610 --> 00:07:11,810
the same post data, but we're just using the get method this time and we're passing the post data to

83
00:07:11,810 --> 00:07:14,510
a params argument instead of data.

84
00:07:16,260 --> 00:07:17,790
And that's it for this code.

85
00:07:17,820 --> 00:07:24,750
It will allow us now to, first of all, extract forms from a specific URL, and it will also allow

86
00:07:24,750 --> 00:07:26,670
us to submit a form.

87
00:07:26,670 --> 00:07:28,710
So this should be called submit form.

88
00:07:29,650 --> 00:07:36,190
By calling this method, given it the form, the value and the URL which we found the form in.

89
00:07:37,240 --> 00:07:38,650
So let's test this.

90
00:07:38,650 --> 00:07:43,140
First of all, let's try to extract this form from this web page.

91
00:07:43,150 --> 00:07:44,860
So I'm just going to copy its link.

92
00:07:45,850 --> 00:07:50,650
And in my program, instead of crawling, I'm actually not going to crawl.

93
00:07:50,660 --> 00:07:59,350
I'm just going to say I want to call my vulnerability scanner that extract forms and I'm going to give

94
00:07:59,350 --> 00:08:05,110
it this URL to extract forms from it, and we're going to run the code.

95
00:08:05,620 --> 00:08:08,860
And I should actually print this because I didn't print it.

96
00:08:08,860 --> 00:08:16,390
So let's say forms are equal to this and let's print my forms.

97
00:08:19,290 --> 00:08:20,010
On Perfect.

98
00:08:20,010 --> 00:08:21,650
As you can see, we have a list.

99
00:08:21,660 --> 00:08:26,490
It contains only one element, which is the form that we see in here.

100
00:08:27,680 --> 00:08:31,850
Next, let's try to test submitting something to this form.

101
00:08:32,240 --> 00:08:41,750
So what I'm going to do is I'm going to say response is equal to vulnerability scanner, dot, submit

102
00:08:41,750 --> 00:08:42,500
form.

103
00:08:43,280 --> 00:08:45,980
We're going to give it the form as the first argument.

104
00:08:45,980 --> 00:08:52,460
So it's going to be my forms zero to select the first element in my list, which is the first form.

105
00:08:53,030 --> 00:08:59,210
We're going to give it the value that we want to submit and let's submit test, test.

106
00:08:59,420 --> 00:09:06,680
And finally, we're going to give it the URL which the form exists in, and it is this URL.

107
00:09:07,550 --> 00:09:13,790
Now this is very rough testing and we're actually going to use these methods in our class later on to

108
00:09:13,790 --> 00:09:15,260
discover vulnerabilities.

109
00:09:15,260 --> 00:09:19,310
So I'm just making sure that these methods that I just build work.

110
00:09:19,700 --> 00:09:24,110
So let's run this again and again.

111
00:09:24,110 --> 00:09:25,490
I forgot to print it.

112
00:09:25,970 --> 00:09:30,500
So we're just going to do a print response, that content.

113
00:09:32,780 --> 00:09:33,680
Run it.

114
00:09:34,950 --> 00:09:40,410
And as you can see, the value that I'm submitting is being submitted because it's saying, hello,

115
00:09:40,410 --> 00:09:41,340
test, test.

116
00:09:41,670 --> 00:09:44,510
And this is basically what happens when you put something in here.

117
00:09:44,520 --> 00:09:51,210
So if you type test, test and submit this, you'll see it will say hello, test, test.

118
00:09:52,140 --> 00:09:57,870
So right now, using our Python program, we're able to extract forms and submit them.

119
00:09:58,770 --> 00:10:04,050
Now, in the next lecture, we're continue building on our scanner and you'll see how we can use it

120
00:10:04,050 --> 00:10:05,820
to discover vulnerabilities.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:01,240 --> 00:00:01,690
Okay.

2
00:00:01,720 --> 00:00:08,260
So now our program is able to get all of the links from all of the pages in my Target website.

3
00:00:08,290 --> 00:00:16,150
It's also able to extract all of the forms, and it's able to submit these forms and send requests to

4
00:00:16,150 --> 00:00:19,120
these links and capture the responses and read them.

5
00:00:19,870 --> 00:00:26,920
So we pretty much have everything except the implementation of the last two steps, which is sending

6
00:00:26,920 --> 00:00:34,420
a request to discover a vulnerability and then analyze the response to check if this vulnerability exists.

7
00:00:35,240 --> 00:00:40,550
So I'm going to build a method called run scanner.

8
00:00:41,180 --> 00:00:48,770
So we're going to do Def Run Scanner, which will actually run the scanner for me.

9
00:00:49,130 --> 00:00:57,860
So first let me expand the constructor and the main goal of this method is to iterate over each link

10
00:00:57,860 --> 00:00:59,780
in my target links list.

11
00:01:00,350 --> 00:01:06,200
And then for each one of these links, extract the forms, test the forms, test the link.

12
00:01:06,200 --> 00:01:09,950
And if you discover anything, then display a message.

13
00:01:10,840 --> 00:01:12,260
So let me show you how to do that.

14
00:01:12,260 --> 00:01:13,480
It's actually very simple.

15
00:01:13,480 --> 00:01:19,330
And keep in mind, I still didn't teach you how to discover vulnerabilities, and I still don't expect

16
00:01:19,330 --> 00:01:21,160
you to know how to do that.

17
00:01:21,820 --> 00:01:23,790
We will talk about that later on.

18
00:01:23,800 --> 00:01:27,820
I'm just trying to show you how generic this program is.

19
00:01:28,770 --> 00:01:36,750
So I'm going to say for each link in my cell, the target links.

20
00:01:38,820 --> 00:01:43,800
I want to, first of all extract all the forms and this link.

21

So I'm going to say forms are equal to self dot extract forms and this method takes a link as an input.

22
00:01:56,970 --> 00:02:03,540
So we're basically using this method right here, which will return a list of all the forms that exist

23
00:02:03,540 --> 00:02:05,640
in the URL that we pass it.

24
00:02:05,640 --> 00:02:12,300
We're passing it one link at a time, and the list of forms will be stored in my forms variable and

25
00:02:12,300 --> 00:02:12,870
here.

26
00:02:14,060 --> 00:02:19,520
Then for each form and this form list so informs.

27
00:02:20,610 --> 00:02:24,710
I want to test this form for vulnerabilities.

28
00:02:24,720 --> 00:02:31,230
So first of all, I'm going to print a message saying testing form in.

29
00:02:33,240 --> 00:02:35,400
And we'll put the link.

30
00:02:36,520 --> 00:02:42,610
And then in here I will start calling methods to discover the vulnerabilities.

31
00:02:42,640 --> 00:02:48,910
Now, so far, we haven't implemented any methods to discover vulnerabilities, but once we do, all

32
00:02:48,910 --> 00:02:51,760
we have to do is literally just call the method in here.

33
00:02:52,820 --> 00:02:59,450
Then once we're done with this loop, this means we're done with testing the forms in the current link,

34
00:02:59,450 --> 00:03:00,950
in the target links.

35
00:03:01,640 --> 00:03:08,720
The next thing that we want to do is to test the link itself, see if this link sends any get requests.

36
00:03:09,440 --> 00:03:17,150
Now we know get requests are sent in the in this form so you'll actually see an equal in the link itself.

37
00:03:17,150 --> 00:03:21,320
And then we usually put our payload after the equal.

38
00:03:21,320 --> 00:03:23,720
So this is where you put the value.

39
00:03:24,590 --> 00:03:27,740
So I'm going to check if equal.

40
00:03:28,660 --> 00:03:30,670
Is in my link.

41
00:03:31,330 --> 00:03:33,730
Then again, I'm going to print.

42
00:03:34,730 --> 00:03:42,110
Testing, followed by the link that we're testing just to tell the user that we are testing this specific

43
00:03:42,110 --> 00:03:42,740
link.

44
00:03:43,040 --> 00:03:51,080
And then again in here we can call all the methods that we will implement later on to discover vulnerabilities

45
00:03:51,080 --> 00:03:52,010
and links.

46
00:03:53,150 --> 00:03:55,610
And that's it for my run scanner method.

47
00:03:56,450 --> 00:03:58,850
So it's a very, very simple method.

48

All it's going to do is it's going to iterate over all the targets that we collect in our crawler for

49
00:04:06,680 --> 00:04:07,380
each link.

50
00:04:07,400 --> 00:04:12,860
First of all, it's going to extract all the forms using our extract forms method.

51

Then it's going to iterate over all of these forms.

52
00:04:16,970 --> 00:04:23,600
For each time we're going to start calling methods to discover vulnerabilities and forms in here.

53

Then once I'm done with testing the forms, we're going to test if the current link contains an equal

54
00:04:30,110 --> 00:04:30,740
sign.

55
00:04:31,100 --> 00:04:36,020
If it does, then this means that this link sends data to the web application.

56
00:04:36,320 --> 00:04:43,340
So in here again, we will call methods to test vulnerabilities and get requests in the URL.

57
00:04:44,240 --> 00:04:50,510
So right now, I'm actually done with this program and you can see how generic this program is.

58
00:04:50,810 --> 00:04:52,550
All we have to do in the future.

Now, as you go and learn about web application vulnerabilities, all you'll have to do is implement

a method here to discover the vulnerability and call it in here.

00:05:02,570 --> 00:05:09,410
If it discovers the vulnerability in a form or in here if it discovers the vulnerability in a link.

63

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:02,840 --> 00:00:09,020
And this lecture and the next few lectures we're going to talk about a vulnerability called excess.

2
00:00:09,440 --> 00:00:12,140
Excess is short for cross-site scripting.

3
00:00:13,130 --> 00:00:19,100
This type of vulnerability allows an attacker to inject JavaScript into a page.

4
00:00:19,920 --> 00:00:26,460
So JavaScript is a programming language and using this vulnerability, an attacker would be able to

5
00:00:26,460 --> 00:00:31,620
execute code written in JavaScript into a certain page, into a website.

6
00:00:33,160 --> 00:00:35,620
JavaScript is a client sign language.

7
00:00:35,620 --> 00:00:42,970
So when the code is executed, it will be executed on the client, on the user, on the person who is

8
00:00:42,970 --> 00:00:44,600
browsing the web page.

9
00:00:44,620 --> 00:00:46,690
It's not going to be executed on the server.

10
00:00:46,690 --> 00:00:53,260
So even if your code results into giving you a reverse shell, the shell will be coming from the user

11
00:00:53,260 --> 00:00:56,920
who is browsing the page and it's not going to be coming from the website.

12
00:00:57,100 --> 00:01:03,850
So any code you write in JavaScript will be exploited or will run on the target user, on the people

13
00:01:03,850 --> 00:01:07,030
who see the pages and not on the web server.

14
00:01:07,570 --> 00:01:12,970
So the web server is only going to be used as means of delivering the code.

15
00:01:14,550 --> 00:01:22,020
There's three main types of excess vulnerabilities persistent or stored, reflected and done based.

16
00:01:23,570 --> 00:01:27,200
The stored excess gets stored into the database.

17
00:01:27,200 --> 00:01:33,470
So the code that you inject will be stored into the database or into the page so that every time any

18
00:01:33,470 --> 00:01:37,070
person views that page, your code will be executed.

19
00:01:38,260 --> 00:01:39,820
And the reflected one.

20
00:01:40,420 --> 00:01:47,920
The code will only be executed when the target user runs a specific URL crafted or written by you,

21
00:01:47,920 --> 00:01:52,240
so you'll be manipulating some sort of URL and send it to a target.

22
00:01:52,240 --> 00:01:57,010
And when they run that target, when they run that URL, the code will be executed.

23
00:01:58,380 --> 00:02:03,750
The Dome based results from JavaScript code written on the client.

24
00:02:03,750 --> 00:02:10,440
So the code will actually be interpreted and run on the client side without having any communication

25
00:02:10,440 --> 00:02:11,550
with the web server.

26
00:02:11,970 --> 00:02:18,510
This could be very dangerous because sometimes web servers apply some sort of security and filtration

27
00:02:18,510 --> 00:02:20,070
to check for XSS.

28
00:02:20,070 --> 00:02:27,390
But with dome based, the code never gets sent to the web server, so the code will be interpreted and

29
00:02:27,390 --> 00:02:32,220
run on the web browser without even interacting with the web server.

30
00:02:32,220 --> 00:02:37,050
And these will be present into websites that update their content without refresh.

31
00:02:37,050 --> 00:02:43,020
So we've all used websites where you put some sort of a string and it does a search without communicating

32
00:02:43,020 --> 00:02:44,010
with the web server.

33
00:02:44,010 --> 00:02:49,440
So if you are able to inject into that, then this injection will not be validated.

34
00:02:49,440 --> 00:02:54,150
So it will be executed straightaway and bypass all validations.

35
00:02:55,760 --> 00:02:59,600
So let's talk about how to discover these kind of vulnerabilities.

36
00:03:00,110 --> 00:03:09,320
The way to do this is you browse through your target and try to inject any text box or any URL that

37
00:03:09,320 --> 00:03:10,780
looks similar to this.

38
00:03:10,790 --> 00:03:16,790
So whenever you see a URL with parameters, try to inject these parameters or try to inject into text

39
00:03:16,790 --> 00:03:17,450
boxes.

40
00:03:18,790 --> 00:03:22,110
So let's have a look on a reflected X example.

41
00:03:22,120 --> 00:03:28,330
So these are the non persistent, non stored vulnerabilities where you have to actually send the code

42
00:03:28,330 --> 00:03:33,310
to the target and once the target runs the code, it will be executed on their machine.

43
00:03:34,590 --> 00:03:35,310
So.

44
00:03:36,230 --> 00:03:39,860
Let's have a look on our DWI website.

45
00:03:40,760 --> 00:03:43,820
I'm just going to make sure the security is set to low.

46
00:03:44,360 --> 00:03:50,720
I'm going to submit this and then we're going to have an example of a reflected excess.

47
00:03:52,330 --> 00:03:58,630
So as you can see now, you can put your name here for this text box and it's just going to say hello.

48
00:03:58,660 --> 00:04:04,780
Now, this is obviously just an example, but the idea is you can inject into text boxes.

49
00:04:04,810 --> 00:04:10,990
Also, if you have a have a look at the URL here, you'll see that it's a get so you can inject into

50
00:04:10,990 --> 00:04:12,400
the URL as well.

51
00:04:12,700 --> 00:04:15,040
So let me start the pad.

52
00:04:18,000 --> 00:04:25,130
And let's try to inject excess code on this and see if the code will be executed or JavaScript code.

53
00:04:25,140 --> 00:04:25,650
Sorry.

54
00:04:25,830 --> 00:04:29,250
So what I'm going to use is a very simple script.

55
00:04:30,540 --> 00:04:33,740
So we're using the script and slash script tag.

56
00:04:33,750 --> 00:04:39,390
Now, there is a lot of ways into discovering these kind of vulnerabilities and a lot of ways to bypass

57
00:04:39,390 --> 00:04:39,990
filters.

58
00:04:39,990 --> 00:04:44,790
But for now, we're just having a look at the basic case where we can inject a normal script.

59
00:04:45,650 --> 00:04:52,610
And I'm going to say alert, which is just a function to give an alert, to give a text box and I'm

60
00:04:52,610 --> 00:05:00,800
going to say X, so I'm going to click on submit and we'll see if this code will be executed.

61
00:05:03,460 --> 00:05:06,670
And as you can see now, it's actually executing my code.

62
00:05:06,670 --> 00:05:14,620
So instead of saying Hello Zayed here it said hello and my code has been executed and it produced this

63
00:05:14,620 --> 00:05:15,580
excess.

65

If you have a look on the URL, it actually already did it for us.

But if you look at the URL here, it's just going to be a bit bigger there.

67

We can see that the name has been obviously all of these characters are just HTML scape characters and

68
00:05:35,620 --> 00:05:43,420
now if you send this URL to anybody, whoever views the URL where the code will be executed on their

69
00:05:43,420 --> 00:05:45,650
machine and it's going to say X.

70
00:05:46,910 --> 00:05:47,990
So let me show you here.

71
00:05:47,990 --> 00:05:50,180
You can also inject this in the URL.

72
00:05:50,180 --> 00:05:52,680
I just want to show you the whole idea of that.

73
00:05:52,700 --> 00:05:57,530
So we have script slash, script access.

74
00:05:59,650 --> 00:06:02,350
If I hit enter, the code will be executed.

75
00:06:02,440 --> 00:06:06,670
So as I said, we can copy this and send it to a certain person.

76
00:06:06,670 --> 00:06:11,170
And once they run that code, this code will be executed on their machine.

77
00:06:13,270 --> 00:06:13,660
Okay.

78
00:06:13,660 --> 00:06:16,840
Now let's have a look on asteroid X example.

79
00:06:17,080 --> 00:06:20,450
Now start X is very similar to a reflected one.

80
00:06:20,560 --> 00:06:24,740
It allows you to inject JavaScript code into the browser.

81
00:06:24,760 --> 00:06:27,850
The code is executed on the people that visit the page.

82
00:06:27,880 --> 00:06:31,000
The only difference is with with the reflected.

83
00:06:31,000 --> 00:06:37,090
You had to send the URL to your target, so the target had to actually click on a URL for the exploit

84
00:06:37,090 --> 00:06:38,950
to run or for the code to run.

85
00:06:39,840 --> 00:06:46,140
And the stored access, the code will actually be stored into the database or into the page.

86
00:06:46,140 --> 00:06:52,320
So every time any person runs that page, they will see your code and your code will be executed.

87
00:06:52,320 --> 00:06:56,160
So you won't need to interact with any users or send them anything.

88
00:06:57,110 --> 00:07:01,040
Therefore, this could be much more dangerous than reflected excesses.

89
00:07:01,880 --> 00:07:03,350
So let's have a look on this now.

90

I clicked on the X stored here on the left and you have this page and this page just allows you to add

91

92

So I'm going to put my name aside.

93

We're just going to do a normal test first and we're going to put a message body, for example, and

94

then I'm going to sign the guest book.

95

And you can see that they added a message called Message Body.

96

So if we go to here.

97

Into another machine.

98

99

100

101

So if we manage to inject code in here, then any person who runs this page, the code will run it without

102

But it serves for the purposes of this video.

111

Access.

But the real magic happens when a normal person.

116

So let's just go home first and let's assume this is just a normal website and people just are coming

117

and browsing it.

And once they're going to go to the guest book, JavaScript code will be executed on their system from

119

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1

Okay.

00:00:01,230 --> 00:00:05,100
So now we know how to discover excess vulnerabilities.

All we are doing is injecting a very simple code that just displays an alert on screen saying that this

5

website is vulnerable.

7

8

the vulnerable pages where we inject our code, we're going to use beef to do this.

9

We had to look on before.

And what we're going to do is we're going to use the beef hook URL and we're going to inject it into

the stored access page so that everybody who visits that page will be hooked to beef, and then we'll

12

here.

2014.

207, which is my current IP.

00:01:24,120 --> 00:01:33,540
This variable is going to equal to self dot test X in form because right now we're iterating over forms

17
00:01:33,750 --> 00:01:41,580
and I'm going to give my form as the input and the link as the link which contains this form.

18
00:01:42,780 --> 00:01:51,390
And then I'm going to test if it's vulnerable to X, then I'm going to print a message saying.

19
00:01:52,730 --> 00:01:57,020
Access discovered in.

20

Link.

21
00:01:59,800 --> 00:02:04,120
And then we'll also say in the following.

22
00:02:04,930 --> 00:02:10,840
Form and then I actually want to print the vulnerable form.

23
00:02:11,820 --> 00:02:18,900
So basically all I'm doing is I'm using a new variable called is vulnerable to excess and I'm setting

24
00:02:18,900 --> 00:02:25,890
the value of this to whatever value returned from the method that tests excess in the form.

25
00:02:26,130 --> 00:02:31,680
Now we know this method returns true if the past form contains a vulnerability.

26
00:02:31,710 --> 00:02:33,900
Otherwise it'll return false.

27
00:02:34,020 --> 00:02:39,900
So if this is true, then I'm going to print that it is vulnerable and I'm going to print the form.

28
00:02:41,470 --> 00:02:44,560
Then I'm actually going to do something similar in here.

29
00:02:44,560 --> 00:02:55,570
So I'm going to say is vulnerable to excess as is equal to self dot test X in link and I'm going to

30
00:02:55,570 --> 00:02:57,820
pass it the link as the input.

31
00:02:58,090 --> 00:03:03,670
Again, this will return either true or false if it returns a true value.

32
00:03:03,670 --> 00:03:07,930
So if is vulnerable to access, then I'm going to print.

33
00:03:10,580 --> 00:03:13,190
Discovered XSS in link.

34
00:03:15,700 --> 00:03:16,630
And that's it.

35
00:03:17,170 --> 00:03:23,620
So you can see how simple it is now to extend my code to discover any type of vulnerability.

36
00:03:24,190 --> 00:03:28,630
The key is to actually write methods, to discover that vulnerability.

37
00:03:28,630 --> 00:03:32,420
And we can just call these methods in my run scanner.

38
00:03:32,440 --> 00:03:36,880
If the method discovers a vulnerability in a form, we can call it here.

39
00:03:36,880 --> 00:03:42,850
If it discovers a vulnerability in a link, we can call it here, and that's it.

40
00:03:43,420 --> 00:03:48,600
Now, what I also want to do is just before we print that, we discovered the vulnerability.

41
00:03:48,610 --> 00:03:50,800

42
00:03:50,800 --> 00:03:54,040
So it's easy for me to find it in the output.

43
00:03:54,940 --> 00:03:58,600
And let's go ahead now to my vulnerability scanner.

44
00:03:58,900 --> 00:04:03,730
And I'm going to remove this because we're actually not going to do this anymore.

45
00:04:03,760 --> 00:04:10,120
The first thing that we're going to do is we're going to crawl and this is going to build my target

46
00:04:10,120 --> 00:04:17,920
list and store all the links in all of the pages in my Target website and the Target Links list.

47
00:04:17,920 --> 00:04:25,660
And then I'm going to call the vulnerability scanner dot run scanner to run the scanner and discover

48
00:04:25,660 --> 00:04:26,890
the vulnerabilities.

49
00:04:27,430 --> 00:04:32,830
And like I said, if you implemented more methods to discover more vulnerabilities, if you put them

50
00:04:32,830 --> 00:04:35,650
in the scanner, this will automatically call them.

51
00:04:35,650 --> 00:04:41,680
So the code here, as you can see, is very simple and all of the heavy work will be done in here.

52
00:04:42,690 --> 00:04:45,180
So let's go ahead and run the code.

53
00:04:45,180 --> 00:04:48,840

And perfect.

55
00:04:51,450 --> 00:04:57,420
Now, if we go up, you can see that the first thing that it was doing is crawling and making a list

56
00:04:57,420 --> 00:04:59,220
of all the possible links.

57
00:04:59,940 --> 00:05:09,510
Then it was testing these links as normal URLs, and then it discovered an X vulnerability in this link.

58
00:05:09,510 --> 00:05:15,660
And we actually have tested this link before and we know it is vulnerable and it's given us the form

59
00:05:15,660 --> 00:05:17,610
that is vulnerable to this.

60
00:05:18,570 --> 00:05:26,100
Then it's testing another link and you can see that it discovered another access vulnerability that

61
00:05:26,100 --> 00:05:27,540
we actually didn't see.

62
00:05:27,540 --> 00:05:31,440
And this is actually a stored access vulnerability.

63
00:05:31,440 --> 00:05:35,280
So whenever it's exploited, it's stored in the web page.

64
00:05:35,280 --> 00:05:40,830
And now anyone who visits this web page will actually see this vulnerability.

65
00:05:41,640 --> 00:05:44,910
So the code will be executed on their browser.

66
00:05:46,060 --> 00:05:53,110
And as you can see, the code is getting executed on my browser because it was injected by this program

67
00:05:53,110 --> 00:05:53,800
in here.

68
00:05:54,280 --> 00:06:00,940
So again, it's given us the form that is vulnerable and then it continues testing other links in which

69

it wasn't able to discover any vulnerabilities.

70
00:06:05,210 --> 00:06:14,030
So basically right now we built a program that can be used to automatically discover vulnerabilities

71
00:06:14,030 --> 00:06:15,660
into any website.

72
00:06:15,680 --> 00:06:19,130
All you have to do is just give it the website, the target.

74

75

76

77

86

89
